name: Mugatu The Rooster
subject: As the commander of Kat Cammack’s chicken coop
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAT FOR CONGRESS
body:
I know chicken sh*t when I see it...%

| | | | [](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zqmro16bfql73hnoaeai3pu8eiochg7ofe897umg_rp22sh2s8i6epb3cth6apj0c3h2bs)
---
---
---
| | Allow me the honor of introducing myself, Friend.
---
| | My name is******Mugatu**. When I was just a cockerel, I found my life purpose in _defending_ _**Rep. Kat Cammack**_ _’s chicken coop_ here in the **FREE** __ state of Florida. _And I’ve spent every waking moment since that day sharpening my combat skills to maintain_ _**LAW & ORDER**_ _within those coop walls._ Taking a page from Kat’s voting playbook in Washington –[ ](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zdmpvsdmsk3rcupnlb94e5drpak8nlvbd97446l8_rp22sh2s8i6epb3cth6apj0c3h2bs)[I __REFUSE__ to allow **foreign invaders** to conquer the land I call home.](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zh5fdg419scs2iskd4q1sd0uii8ichqfa3lk8rl8_rp22sh2s8i6epb3cth6apj0c3h2bs) So my message to any _fox_ , _hawk_ , _snake_ or even _ALLIGATOR_ thinking about trespassing is quite simple: **“FAAFO.”**
---
| | [](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zm0ulk150gsmsb79k22dj52om6ri15lntksegajg_rp22sh2s8i6epb3cth6apj0c3h2bs)
---
---

| | **_Meet Kat_** Kat Cammack is currently serving her third term representing the FREE state of Florida in the People’s House as FL-03 Congresswoman. And her road from the small cattle ranch she grew up on to the halls of Congress in our nation’s capital has been far from ordinary. A third generation commercial sandblaster, Kat originally aspired to take over her family’s business and continue the rural, rodeo lifestyle she had grown so accustomed to. That was until a failed Obama-era housing policy caused Kat’s family to lose their ranch, leaving her and her mother homeless. They lived in their car and bounced from cheap motel to cheap motel just to keep a roof over their heads. But overcoming these hardships moulded Kat into the gritty and unapologetic leader she is today. After her family’s livelihood was destroyed by big government, she made it her life mission to deliver federal reform in the DC Swamp for the American people. So she packed her belongings and moved to Florida’s 3rd Congressional District – where she would serve as a Deputy Chief of Staff for nearly a decade before running for Congress herself. After emerging victorious in a brutal, 10-way GOP primary contest, the people of FL-03 elected Kat to serve them in the People’s House in November of 2020. Throughout her journey from homelessness to the House of Representatives, Kat Cammack has shown unwavering and unapologetic grit in her fight for the American people. And she has no plans of backing down until we truly have a government for the people, by the people once again here in the United States. When she’s not going toe-to-toe with the Washington Swamp, Kat enjoys spending time with her loving husband Matt – who serves as a firefighter and SWAT Medic with the Gainesville Fire Rescue. In the little spare time that they have, you can find Kat and Matt fishing, skeet shooting, or watching Gators football. They are incredibly proud to call north central Florida’s “Gator Nation” home. Go Gators, and let’s keep Florida the most FREE state in the union!

body:
Reactivate fat loss with GLP1

It's not celebrities.

It's not influencers.

And it's definitely not another fad diet.

The people losing the most weight right now are everyday Americans — moms, dads, teachers, and grandparents — who stumbled on a little-known "Golden Switch" that reactivates fat-burning deep inside their cells.

* A mom of three slipped back into jeans she hadn't worn in 15 years.

* A retired firefighter dropped 41 pounds and says he feels more energized than he did in his 30s.

* A 62-year-old grandmother lost 36 pounds and now runs circles around her grandkids.

The best part? Almost nobody knows about it yet.

name: Rudy Giuliani
subject: The People’s Pardon Of Rudy Giuliani
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund Legal Defense Trust
body:
My last chance. My Hail Mary.

| [](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zbs6t6q3qilku0nars5m4qat0c1tlt6cssgvieq8_rp22sh2s8i6epb3cth69o70clh2bs)
---
Friend,
---
Believe me, writing this letter was not my first choice...
---
_As a matter of fact, it’s my _last_ _resort_. _
---
**You see, I’m writing to you as a man who’s run out of time, resources, and options ahead of the TRIAL OF MY LIFE.**
---
**A Democrat Attorney General is making me stand in a rigged trial for bogus state-level crimes, because she knows that not even the most powerful man on the face of the planet – President Trump – can save me with a _federal_ pardon in _state_ court.**
---
And so, today, I’m not reaching out as America’s Mayor, not as a former prosecutor, not even as President Trump’s former attorney, but as a grandfather and a patriot who’s staring down a 20-year prison sentence for doing what I believed – _and KNOW_ – was RIGHT.
---
The Democrats and their Deep State goons think they’ve finally got me. They’ve left me with no escape, no lifeline – **especially after they’ve already STRIPPED me of my law licenses!**
---
_No viable pardon from the President.
No protection.
No cavalry coming over the hill.
_
---
But even though the most powerful man in the country legally cannot step in to save me, the most powerful _force_ absolutely can: _THE AMERICAN PEOPLE_.
---
So, with my back against the wall, I’m turning to the one thing that can save me:**_the collective voices of MILLIONS of patriots like _YOU_._**
---
My mugshot was entered under booking #2313757, so my goal is for 2,313,757 patriots to sign my official petition — [**“THE PEOPLE’S PARDON OF RUDY GIULIANI”**](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z955uils8v5vv4dke1po4iipb2dihl175iu7d810_rp22sh2s8i6epb3cth69o70clh2bs)— calling for my freedom and to DROP ALL CHARGES.
---
**_...And we’re nearly there!_**
---
[**So please, help me reach this life-saving (_truly!_) goal today by immediately adding your name to “THE PEOPLE’S PARDON” as citizens unite to call for my freedom and to DROP ALL CHARGES against me. **](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zknshrqqcf6p2a6dp3v19uiqp62hgorehmoh8gko_rp22sh2s8i6epb3cth69o70clh2bs)
---

__

Thank you and God bless you,

clean_body: My last chance. My Hail Mary. | Friend, Believe me, writing this letter was not my first choice... As a matter of fact, it’s my last resort. You see, I’m writing to you as a man who’s run out of time, resources, and options ahead of the TRIAL OF MY LIFE. A Democrat Attorney General is making me stand in a rigged trial for bogus state-level crimes, because she knows that not even the most powerful man on the face of the planet – President Trump – can save me with a federal pardon in state court. And so, today, I’m not reaching out as America’s Mayor, not as a former prosecutor, not even as President Trump’s former attorney, but as a grandfather and a patriot who’s staring down a 20-year prison sentence for doing what I believed – and KNOW – was RIGHT. The Democrats and their Deep State goons think they’ve finally got me. They’ve left me with no escape, no lifeline – especially after they’ve already STRIPPED me of my law licenses! No viable pardon from the President. No protection. No cavalry coming over the hill. But even though the most powerful man in the country legally cannot step in to save me, the most powerful force absolutely can: THE AMERICAN PEOPLE. So, with my back against the wall, I’m turning to the one thing that can save me:the collective voices of MILLIONS of patriots like YOU. My mugshot was entered under booking #2313757, so my goal is for 2,313,757 patriots to sign my official petition — — calling for my freedom and to DROP ALL CHARGES. ...And we’re nearly there! Thank you and God bless you, Rudy Giuliani Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund Paid for by Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund Legal Defense Trust Pop Acta Media | Your America PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522 |

name: Donald John Trump
subject: I want to try and get to Heaven.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trump National Committee JFC, Inc
body:
I believe He saved me for a reason, and this is why I think He did it!

| | | | [](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b59K/?__vbtrk=MzE0NzU4OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzE0NzU4OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
---
John,
**I want to try and get to Heaven.**
Last year I came millimeters from death when that bullet pierced through my skin. My triumphant return to the White House was never supposed to happen!
But I believe that God saved me for one reason: **TO MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN!**
I wasn’t supposed to beat Crooked Hillary in 2016 — but I did.
I wasn’t supposed to secure the border & build the greatest economy in history — but I did.
I certainly wasn’t supposed to survive an assassin’s bullet — but by the grace of the almighty God, I did.
SO NOW, I have no other choice but to answer the Call to Duty, but I can’t do it alone.
John, you’ve been with me through everything.
You were there when they launched: the Russia Hoax, the first Impeachment, the second Impeachment, the rigged 2020 election, the Raid on Mar-a-Lago, the Arrest and Mugshot at the Fulton County Jail, the Removal from the CO ballot, the sham cases in DC / FL / NY / DC, the first Assassination attempt, the second Assassination attempt, and so much more!
Just because I won in a HISTORIC landslide and have unleashed the Golden Age of America, **THAT DOESN’T MEAN WE CAN TAKE OUR FOOT OFF THE GAS!**
The forces that hate our country don’t want a secure border or safe cities or high paying jobs. The Radical Left Lunatics want to STOP MAGA, but we’re not going to let them NO MATTER WHAT.
**Let me make this SOLEMN PLEDGE to you: I WILL NEVER SURRENDER!**
I put my life on the line willingly because this is what I was made to do, but our movement, the beautiful MAGA movement, is powered by YOU.
Every time they count us out – count YOU out – we come back bigger, better, and stronger than ever before.
This is our moment to show the world that we will NEVER back down, NEVER give an inch, and NEVER SURRENDER in our fight to save this country.
_That’s why I’ve launched a 24 HOUR TRUMP FUNDRAISING BLITZ, and I’m asking everyone to chip in $15 to make it one for the record books!_
**_[So if you’re with me all the way to the end, join with MILLIONS of your fellow PATRIOTS by chipping in $15 to keep the wins rolling in!>](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b59K/?__vbtrk=MzE0NzU4OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzE0NzU4OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_**
[GIVE $15](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b59K/?__vbtrk=MzE0NzU4OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzE0NzU4OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
[STAND WITH TRUMP](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b59K/?__vbtrk=MzE0NzU4OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzE0NzU4OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
Sincerely,
Donald John Trump

## Chicago Readies for National Guard Deployment
Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson issued an [executive order](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L3unDAQBPCnMR1ovebrChdp6P5pU6I9vIY9GYyMOcTbR-QV0o_mp5k_P-MPSZBt_k0r2-zjuPF1BM6Z05hOd89yBqFNZPuciV1eEvMxImBTwqsEPTqScI914axnRjYFW901bYdaN3WxWPPq333rfdsa6N_ASH4icHXH02S8g0Ls0wYv0BqgabAi7antCTWDaTruVQ3sJMdUfaJseWEf7vjlVE1xLYJdct4PZX4pHBQO13VVewySZYpPQOHwLFI4PIrCAXqFgwGFw7TIRHMsV7pjKnM6173cKEvcKJTzScmVAI3pdd0Vybr9khDkaDtVw7yShH9-_q_Tvhb_BgAA____Y3-R) Saturday to ensure law enforcement in the Windy City remains subject to the Chicago Police Department, not the federal troops President Trump has hinted he will deploy there. The president has yet to confirm a deployment with Illinois Governor JB Pritzker (D), who has labeled any such action as an "invasion." Meanwhile, [Trump criticized Pritzker](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGS3CAQRL8GZWwNo0XSBgROyM6pQxUWgzRnBCpAtyV_vUv-hYu76nX3-_UxfziOnNafbifTQp4TvWuk1qjM5fTXymdkl5jT51nIt60Q1RkBtYSXBDV7x_Gan503gQip78ioUQ8jKqWf3WYUKJo8OqXBh5fq8bdC0tQHCAoG8B2bmwYvUApAa3w4FdwwOVQEvR5pEk8gzy2Xx2fm1DYK8cpfVB5L3rtottaOKvofAq1Am64j13ZHAu0NFmhhEmh7JdCeVd7_BNpWzv2QNbq9yqNw-_uHisxJLhsvbs1yKbyTXO-elEuVhY5cGnn5Jl63JmOuVYaTYpUIOMl60HJG1zgngbYrxh9vjpHrMIonrLvj-H9v-5bkL4P_AgAA__92VpHX) on social media over Chicago's crime and the city's status as having one of the nation's highest homicide rates. Reports have surfaced that up to 250 federal troops have been assigned to Chicago. Border Czar Tom Homan confirmed this week that "[a large contingent](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLEOmzAQhp_GbI7MGQcYGCpVbOnaEV3wGV9qMDJHorx9RV-h8yd9d__3-zE9kBNvyy9caZCQp40-RyIRKlM5_XfhMzFuzNvrLOQlFqJjAgNOm16bevLI6Ts1lR8CEZCtaKhbd2-hrl1TxcFY-3T4RDv3TWgAOuxcbwmC7y2YABUPl830pq6NcQ5uWAe8dwg1Geta6lRjyLPkcntl3iRSSN_8pnKb81qlIYrsh7I_FIwKRokUOaWLKRhjXukapGA8BIX0B2WOCkbXtB20oH3eMHkt5Vx3vefEM2nBP3T59YbCF9fLicXrOfKMS9ZXA5TrnB3lWSgo-zPuVRn8_uGU-Li3qjHLivzvjUr-q-t7gL8BAAD__wXujc0)" of ICE officers would conduct large-scale deportation operations there.
## Rudy Giuliani Injured in Freak Car Accident
Former New York City Mayor Rudy Giuliani was [seriously injured](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D1uxCAQhuHT4I4VDP7bgiIN3aZNaREztmcXgwXYK98-cqScIPVIr-b5vh7Dw5KnMH_aFXWZ4hDwnT2WgmlIuztn2j3ZQBSee0JXloSYBxDQcHHnQg7Okj-HunJ6QgRUFWrZNW0HUjZ1tWiFWAuQKHulXK1GUM46Z9V3i9D2DivSV03chZRCNA3crJxs21uQKFTTYc9qgY5KTLdnpFAWnPwZD0y3Ma6V10spW2bqg4FhYMK5xVyuEwNzhRkY0TMwSjIwe-aXj4G5bPwPx5c9FU6Bjzbxd8Lxxe1UMPEF_UZh5i6umAuN_KDoMYzIDxoLrTxv8YV5tYGBqZJ225u8p9x2rBbzasn_Pln-teyh4ScAAP__8UOOmw) in a car accident in New Hampshire on Saturday night, according to his spokesman, Michael Ragusa. Ragusa said Giuliani's rental car was struck from behind at high speed, resulting in a [broken vertebra](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHOozAQBOCngc7IXmNsChfXpMu1V6INXuLNGYiMEytvf-L0_0_w96MZffPnOl2RE2_337iSL8s-bVSPRKVQnvIrfO78Sowb8_Z4ZQolZqJjAglGyFFINQXk9Jn6NviFCEi35JU1gwWlTN9GHwjRDcY6jTc1WmfVom8KrJ5n48bFtezPNjlKpaQ0BjpUCw4OQZHUxpJrekmBy567x85bibSkz_6m3M372iYfS3kejf7VwKWBS621OwmV6O8ZaOByMsS3Q3xBxIxZzBmPKDaqIuL6PCJnEqAA9Gja7MOzckp8DLbp5X1FTv8Xy49uenv4FwAA__8dg30O) and multiple bruises and cuts. Ragusa explained that Giuliani was flagged down by a victim of domestic violence, stayed with the person until police arrived, and was hit after reentering his rental car. Giuliani will likely remain in the Manchester-area hospital for two or three more days, but is said to be recovering and "[in good spirits](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGOwyAMBuCnIVsi4wBpBoZbsvXWGxENTuOKJhXQRn37E5XuCW6zZMu_vv_n7M6eI2_Xb38nW5bdbXTkSKVQcukZ3ld-RvYb83Z7JgplTUTZIaBuYWxBuuA5vp1qgl2IkPqGrBy0GVBKrZrVakPaYLicAo2IvRqVIjQBetAE5EPDtn6DEaQE0Bo7LxdvTh4lQa8HOgkFFLjsqbvtvJWVlvjeX5S6eb830a6lPLLovwROAqfjOLr5kqui7gVOdRQ4VUz7p2lnn9o5-by2HxhTPWmSDY-DY-RsBqHgevccPynlX9W8LP4GAAD__z7bePo)."
## Biggest US Recessions by Duration
The U.S. has experienced 34 recessions since 1855, but they've become shorter and less frequent in recent decades. [See a chart here](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLtuhDAQAL_GdCB7wTwKF2noLm1KZOH1sSefjewFxN9Hl19IP5qR5uexPCwFis9v-0bDPi0RrxKQGfOSD3c_6QhkI1F8HRkdbxmxLCBB13KqpVqcpXAvXeWMRwRsKzRq0P0ASumu2swwrXJ0a6d755SHVg2dnkZ0FrxUY6srMh-bnKRSUmoNjVXe9qMFhbLVA46ik-iIU25eiSJv6MOdTszNmt5VMBvzXkT7JWAWMF_X1ZxUDhtWuxPbQIU_oIB53QXMGxVO-a6Tr49SZ1yxFEqxCJirbNx-UQhU-kF08vm2FP4i_K8zp4HfAAAA___apnjQ). Before World War II, recessions occurred roughly every four years and could last over two years, but since 1945, the average length has shrunk to just 10 months. Read about the [main recessions here](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOhCAURb8GuzHwVMCCYhu72XZLg_Ac3wTBADNm_n7j_sJ2t7g5Jzk_9_luKVB8fNsdTV3THPEsAWvFPOeX_zzoFchGovh8ZfR1y4hlBg7DjY83LmZvKXzmvvFmRQTsGjRCDVKBEEPfbGZRcpW95zDKEWWnxk4rpZdOew2LU0tD5qLxkQvB-TBAa8VqpbYgkHeDQs16jp5qyu0zUawbruGT3phbl_YmmK3Wo7Dui8HEYDrPs6X4xlLTgZ7sdWIw2VzJBSwMJnQppp3ctblmMB221FtGh6VQiqW15Wiy8cdJIVCRivX8sVsKf776r0hvA78BAAD__85dfE4). The Panic of 1873 lasted 65 months, while the COVID-19 recession in 2020 lasted only two. Improved monetary policy, automatic stabilizers like unemployment insurance, and quicker federal intervention have helped soften economic blows. Today, downturns rarely escalate into full depressions, marking a major shift in how the U.S. economy weathers financial storms.

Biggest US Recessions by Duration The U.S. has experienced 34 recessions since 1855, but they've become shorter and less frequent in recent decades. . Before World War II, recessions occurred roughly every four years and could last over two years, but since 1945, the average length has shrunk to just 10 months. Read about the . The Panic of 1873 lasted 65 months, while the COVID-19 recession in 2020 lasted only two. Improved monetary policy, automatic stabilizers like unemployment insurance, and quicker federal intervention have helped soften economic blows. Today, downturns rarely escalate into full depressions, marking a major shift in how the U.S. economy weathers financial storms. name: L.t. Gen. Michael Flynn
subject: The Deep State wanted to destroy me
body:
Did Barack Obama commit treason?| | |
---
| |
---
| | Dear Fellow Patriot,**Did Barack Obama commit treason when he launched a multi-front campaign to destroy Donald Trump, his allies, and cripple his incoming Administration?** My answer is “Yes” because I was personally and professionally destroyed by them.Barack Obama along with Hillary Clinton, Susan Rice, James Comey, and other power-hungry radicals concocted this scheme from inside the White House.But the real conspiracy wasn’t just behind closed doors.Everyone was in on it – especially and most dangerously the mainstream media. I’m talking about 24/7 hit pieces. The never-ending barrage of mysterious leaks. Their conspiracy was made possible by collaborators inside the FBI like James Comey and the House Intelligence Committee like Adam Schiff who lied to your face that they had evidence to “get” Trump.This is why I’ve partnered with my friends at the American Media Council to make sure we can reshape the media landscape before the next hoax can destroy our country. **[Today, I’m asking you to join me in supporting their critical mission.](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=2de268e38d334cb59e24d112f380b15d&_e=aHBzf3u6-Viba_Cg0tY7bOXcoxXX2SsFIOrIgQTO8K7UiPmG2Ass6P3LgcePuHYFR77TzglgsHVffaz17yuE4Okb0HzvtqK4aymXBsq5zOuHYpZGhHmHDIcqy6kUC27jcxQ2usxz5xwb2Cb49ODizEhdyEeYsOb0EWEWFV4SajU8PA1og5ndn3c9Bb0RwjoHBEgJAY7rjdCTgDIOLRCqFzJiyaEd1CKl45k7u_ZvUHTdtkJc4ZcmxjX_J0BCFp3oU4hmPp7qGYDFb5wYyVpsYDBeoOuK8ppGCd4zjpMfGocqTBTViHL-Yck0Pk6XVudvMBHGrpG2XIhE5C9q4Z0qn8KRTr8T8B8HdoGkO-BISUO4MxfHY4GcEAWHdJFDtS2LW52kSwPGKMYulPtv0wXBQxnPhKAprLVdupkFiOXCxm1YMnJQHt57GfBhdLf83ldJn7YILckpVpK_i-fzDq4zjpLMfv6EaJ2B9cJ_izPuNL-Zik_q6WCh5CnXn6N_LX4zkJRRxVcCbOj6k33bq1ym8srAlgftxyfJcU93-wbXT74inZ763lelSwmUBqOgyo7o9vLmRVTQc3cqMkNmZ3OCoRC5NVlZ4HNDQdMCiR-b3hZmSsn7rx4U0QADUy2cn6u4KE_CpEEfVdDpvzLGN2K16YFxw9UB9Ky_kpVkSzmIfFgx1EW9uhdQXP316mx26jTSf1fISBe76uO9NRXVQisf0H1PZjjLwnZRq0MRPTJ2MTjdXbTIAG7DR4mTn6VGq4B_5NDGsyR-T3RKtthzrut3gg%3D%3D)** It’s a mission I care deeply about because I was the target of the Fake News’ lies.Here’s the truth: Obama and Hillary didn’t have a lick of evidence when they cooked up this hoax. Here’s the _disturbing_ truth: they didn’t need any.They could make up whatever they wanted to and feed it to the mainstream media monster. That’s exactly what they did.Let me explain:
---
1. They planted fake evidence in the mainstream media.

2. The mainstream media ran them as if they were fact.

3. The mainstream media's coverage was cited as proof the fake evidence was real.

---
It was terrible circular logic built on a mountain of lies.**But it worked.** It worked because the liberal media drilled it into the minds of millions of Americans that President Trump was some kind of traitor.The whole time, these Trump-hating journalists celebrated their role in making this hoax a reality. The Washington Post even won a Pulitzer Prize over it!The level to which the liberal media went to justify this insanity was insane itself.That’s why today I’m honored to partner with a group who’s fighting to stop another hoax like this from destroying our country.The American Media Council understands what’s really going on:**The power-hungry radicals in Washington who have weaponized our government have also weaponized the mainstream media.** Their mission is to restore truth in the media before the Fake News can destroy our country. They’re the reason I’m able to write you this. What I deeply respect about the American Media Council is that they aren’t sitting back and watching it happen. They’re on the frontlines trying to make a difference.
---
* They publish the facts behind top media hoaxes.
* They hold journalists accountable with their “Truth in Journalism” Pledge.
* They give out “Trustworthy Ratings” to help patriots like you know which sites you can trust.

---
But that’s not all.Thanks to the generous support of patriots like you, they have a **“First Amendment Fund”** to help conservatives overcome lawfare waged by the mainstream media. **[Today, will you consider filling their “First Amendment Fund” with a donation of $35 or more?](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=2de268e38d334cb59e24d112f380b15d&_e=aHBzf3u6-Viba_Cg0tY7bOXcoxXX2SsFIOrIgQTO8K7UiPmG2Ass6P3LgcePuHYFR77TzglgsHVffaz17yuE4Okb0HzvtqK4aymXBsq5zOuHYpZGhHmHDIcqy6kUC27jcxQ2usxz5xwb2Cb49ODizEhdyEeYsOb0EWEWFV4SajU8PA1og5ndn3c9Bb0RwjoHBEgJAY7rjdCTgDIOLRCqFzJiyaEd1CKl45k7u_ZvUHTdtkJc4ZcmxjX_J0BCFp3oU4hmPp7qGYDFb5wYyVpsYP_YtUPW-rQHiAPy4Hcbs-trvVDDEWAOYUPCW0jGpytfhz6UKQMRnbVoEcydZZ8tAG8T8ksBJxzPp828sA9ULRsIg1i5LWzMLwg0kuSYwy_GJlosoT14o3bd9ZMNdmGVT1WRBeMqASDf2-GGiknjgEGAL6G2_QRrax195GFGYFBf_EAxlyyreD3xKCizxGJIn1khLRJNMV1HyGeiRLs6UMpAvDN3PkpR6mFD_9QTJyhpuago7bJ0sgUIy9Z93eb5TqlvN-cMM6aELHJDZ6dxw_wTSm8RHNn7fD5alyWWGljyxK4jOcOKnXkm1X7a5JNFgjHdvWpkZcC-ornDZFjO6PYFrISCOxc45_06JzZrs_zwh5i6WGhhU775uH69dwUcL9bZrSCFZ-zPncru2pnhlitvy3LIG1rziIc3pePi_BMsdUFunESN-a6k_lex7cd0qu3ACPGqusmaWgbYdqwtJKKhiEbuMkPHxE1EvHUHqshjoO8FB4V0DRgTA7AhO6wVtw%3D%3D)** A $35 donation would be a tremendous help. But if you feel called to make a monthly donation, say $15 or more, I will send you a complimentary copy of Pardon of Innocence.It’s so important to me that you support the American Media Council that I want to “Thank You” with a complimentary book.My friend, it’s hard to put into words just how impactful your support will be today...... but I’ll try.The Deep State wanted to destroy me.They tried to take away my career and financially bankrupt my family. I had to sell my house to pay the endless legal fees. It put my wife, Lori, through hell and back.That’s the Left’s goal.**Humiliation. Retaliation. Destruction.**Sadly, the damage to my reputation can’t be fixed in a courtroom or wiped from the internet.President Donald Trump pardoned me. My name has been officially cleared. But no matter where I go... certain people will always see me the way the liberal media painted me.That’s why the Left pushes these hoaxes. It’s all about inflicting **maximum punishment** on anybody who dares to stand up against their radical doctrine.Your support of the American Media Council and their **“First Amendment Fund”** today will directly help patriots like me clear their names and stand up against the liberal media’s lies.This is only possible with your support today. **[Will you support the American Media Council just like I do?](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=2de268e38d334cb59e24d112f380b15d&_e=aHBzf3u6-Viba_Cg0tY7bOXcoxXX2SsFIOrIgQTO8K7UiPmG2Ass6P3LgcePuHYFR77TzglgsHVffaz17yuE4Okb0HzvtqK4aymXBsq5zOuHYpZGhHmHDIcqy6kUC27jcxQ2usxz5xwb2Cb49ODizEhdyEeYsOb0EWEWFV4SajU8PA1og5ndn3c9Bb0RwjoHBEgJAY7rjdCTgDIOLRCqFzJiyaEd1CKl45k7u_ZvUHTdtkJc4ZcmxjX_J0BCFp3oU4hmPp7qGYDFb5wYyVpsYG-MOX7e0WTkJ1wmVMfnrzylFKjapOk6odCzL9A1zQ2AcoJ-ye8qVXUrS2zanv3BavONj7m60R6seYmwFvO6ZALVPwEWJGheAFBc7aiW8rd-j0fdndevQToRCUvzHR9SHje_Y-wyTmZ7N-zMYkAMpMQnJ0CDgbYnk8ObYVp5OUtNrRRI9ZYj3A_QQACNOGXpunLAOT_hX50NIAe6ZtETjrM5iNBzqF1KkNGTmJtXRj8I1mjT8mXSZTnFivDKXPS0UlkVUaVCMtbPGWL_xkvR6JUAVkiWp2H_w1RKUbUIwENDfgYWCBC5AclEfloPYCxx24ZMgfWL-zJP3Gs8atftMtwfWfAdMacbJTA1MLuiVUoBnSeLHgZbffMOrqLvusyIzZFP8qnZYlmTw4X7zCQGBG5VDEvvfz6axMt6ENR0ki83QDrF4jdR9S6dDG983BET__IHrTmUE1nrAALu4NWKvGUvSnDP1z-G60i9xTQ8hrjd4ve6m2AGDEHTJ-TtCLH5iQ%3D%3D)** If you’re in a position to sign up for a monthly donation today – $15 or more – I will send you a complimentary copy of Pardon of Innocence as my sincere thanks.
---
Thank You and God Bless You
---
Lt. Gen. Michael Flynn
---

clean_body: Did Barack Obama commit treason?| | | | | | | Dear Fellow Patriot,Did Barack Obama commit treason when he launched a multi-front campaign to destroy Donald Trump, his allies, and cripple his incoming Administration? My answer is “Yes” because I was personally and professionally destroyed by them.Barack Obama along with Hillary Clinton, Susan Rice, James Comey, and other power-hungry radicals concocted this scheme from inside the White House.But the real conspiracy wasn’t just behind closed doors.Everyone was in on it – especially and most dangerously the mainstream media. I’m talking about 24/7 hit pieces. The never-ending barrage of mysterious leaks. Their conspiracy was made possible by collaborators inside the FBI like James Comey and the House Intelligence Committee like Adam Schiff who lied to your face that they had evidence to “get” Trump.This is why I’ve partnered with my friends at the American Media Council to make sure we can reshape the media landscape before the next hoax can destroy our country. It’s a mission I care deeply about because I was the target of the Fake News’ lies.Here’s the truth: Obama and Hillary didn’t have a lick of evidence when they cooked up this hoax. Here’s the disturbing truth: they didn’t need any.They could make up whatever they wanted to and feed it to the mainstream media monster. That’s exactly what they did.Let me explain: 1. They planted fake evidence in the mainstream media. 2. The mainstream media ran them as if they were fact. 3. The mainstream media's coverage was cited as proof the fake evidence was real. It was terrible circular logic built on a mountain of lies.But it worked. It worked because the liberal media drilled it into the minds of millions of Americans that President Trump was some kind of traitor.The whole time, these Trump-hating journalists celebrated their role in making this hoax a reality. The Washington Post even won a Pulitzer Prize over it!The level to which the liberal media went to justify this insanity was insane itself.That’s why today I’m honored to partner with a group who’s fighting to stop another hoax like this from destroying our country.The American Media Council understands what’s really going on:The power-hungry radicals in Washington who have weaponized our government have also weaponized the mainstream media. Their mission is to restore truth in the media before the Fake News can destroy our country. They’re the reason I’m able to write you this. What I deeply respect about the American Media Council is that they aren’t sitting back and watching it happen. They’re on the frontlines trying to make a difference. They publish the facts behind top media hoaxes. They hold journalists accountable with their “Truth in Journalism” Pledge. They give out “Trustworthy Ratings” to help patriots like you know which sites you can trust. But that’s not all.Thanks to the generous support of patriots like you, they have a “First Amendment Fund” to help conservatives overcome lawfare waged by the mainstream media. A $35 donation would be a tremendous help. But if you feel called to make a monthly donation, say $15 or more, I will send you a complimentary copy of Pardon of Innocence.It’s so important to me that you support the American Media Council that I want to “Thank You” with a complimentary book.My friend, it’s hard to put into words just how impactful your support will be today...... but I’ll try.The Deep State wanted to destroy me.They tried to take away my career and financially bankrupt my family. I had to sell my house to pay the endless legal fees. It put my wife, Lori, through hell and back.That’s the Left’s goal.Humiliation. Retaliation. Destruction.Sadly, the damage to my reputation can’t be fixed in a courtroom or wiped from the internet.President Donald Trump pardoned me. My name has been officially cleared. But no matter where I go... certain people will always see me the way the liberal media painted me.That’s why the Left pushes these hoaxes. It’s all about inflicting maximum punishment on anybody who dares to stand up against their radical doctrine.Your support of the American Media Council and their “First Amendment Fund” today will directly help patriots like me clear their names and stand up against the liberal media’s lies.This is only possible with your support today. If you’re in a position to sign up for a monthly donation today – $15 or more – I will send you a complimentary copy of Pardon of Innocence as my sincere thanks. Thank You and God Bless You Lt. Gen. Michael Flynn P.S. As my deepest thanks for supporting the American Media Council and their critical mission, I want to send you a complimentary copy of my new book, Pardon of Innocence. name: Team Evergreen
subject: Where labor and climate meet
body:
Clean energy jobs 🤝 good-paying jobs
| | | | [](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3931065/87SdQ4wZ) |
---|---|---
---
| Happy Labor Day, friend!

Today, we honor the American labor movement and celebrate the contributions that working people make each and every day towards building a better future.

We believe that climate and labor movements are inextricably linked in the fight for that future—one where no one has to choose between a good-paying job and a healthy environment.

There are numerous benefits of a clean energy economy for the workforce. For one, it improves conditions for workers, particularly those who are exposed to polluted air or extreme temperatures while on the job.

Clean energy is also creating good, union jobs. **It’s generating _more jobs per dollar invested_ than the fossil fuel industry, which has been firing workers while cashing in record profits.**

[**Share our Instagram post to spread the word.**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3931066/87SdQ4wZ)**** 👇 |
[ ](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3931067/87SdQ4wZ)
---
---
| It’s possible to build an economy that works for all of us. In doing so, we can take back power from the ultrarich fossil fuel CEOs and corporations that are sucking workers dry and endangering our future on this planet.

There’s so much work to do, but rest and reflection are important too. So if you have the day off, please enjoy—and thank you for all that you do! |
---
| In solidarity,

Team Evergreen |
---
| **P.S.** Authoritarians fear organized workers. We’re proud that our Evergreen staff are unionized—strong unions are how we build a better future. |
---
* * *
---
| _Evergreen is building the ambitious, actionable policy roadmap for an all-out mobilization to defeat climate change—and to create millions of jobs in a thriving, just, and inclusive clean energy future. None of this would be possible without your support._ |
---
[DONATE](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3931068/87SdQ4wZ)

name: Katherine Clark
subject: Billionaires before babies
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action PAC
body:
The new GOP party line:
| | | | |
---
| | | | |
---
| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T4c5ec0bd-0a4d-4d96-93a8-ad4146a09bd8/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
| Billionaires before babies. That’s the GOP’s new party line, Peter. Their budget bill gives a $4.5 trillion tax break to the ultra-wealthy — while expecting working families to foot the bill. When kids don’t have food on their table, it’ll be because of Republicans. When pregnant women can’t find a doctor in their area, it’ll be because of Republicans. And when nursing homes close because they don’t have the funding to stay open, it’ll be because of Republicans. Our only option now is to take back the House and send a powerful message to the GOP that Blue Amp Action and I won’t stand for this. Our families won’t stand for this. Our country won’t stand for this. [**Will you chip in $10 and make sure that my campaign and Blue Amp Action have the resources to take back the House? Every contribution gets us one step closer to securing a brighter future for families across America.**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tb6cf3dd9-bbb9-495d-8db5-4862c99298ce/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | | | I’m not standing by while Donald Trump and his MAGA allies try to line the pockets of the wealthy at the expense of our families. I’m getting ready to ensure we have a plan to fight back and hold the Trump administration and their GOP lackeys in Congress accountable. [It all starts with flipping the House blue, Peter. If you’re with me and Blue Amp Action, contribute $10 or whatever you can now.](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tdb747b4f-620d-4451-9ab8-31725182bad4/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T24ac3c3e-9a9e-455f-884f-0ffc341fe5c0/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: The Texas Flyover
subject: 10-Year Road Map, TCU vs. UNC, and a Rowdy Waterspout
body:
TxDOT’s 2026 Unified Transportation Program was [adopted last month](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkTGu5CAMhk9DOkZAGEiKFCut6N62W0ZOMBMkEiLwm7zcfsVcYTvLtv7Pn_z3a_6CmOLx-gM7TvQT8nzgVRMSYZmluBFKyeB3OGn9ftfvY4XDw6yEenIxciFnDzHds-78tAg_yg4naZ_GKq0H022THoZgZG-VNVb6MSy9GG2_GqHC4ke_dnFqYWIUUkohlXg8lzFoa-y6oEIEYFqgj5TLgzYk_IEa0p3fWB5r3rs0bURnZf0vphxT7rquB-xQYkq5LTDlKuVyM-WaGVOu4ZhyYmi1ZMp9MvmrIB48xddGlUtt-BJTivngPqcEhVOBo565EFDrngkOTpnH_Sz5jW0cQlw5rCXXyisBIVNueFqjR2WEsO2-3tFSMLD-93Z2ZfLn1SDVWKbFa4eYPk70f594T-pfAAAA__-dVZxg). It provides a 10-year road map for more than $146 billion in improvement projects and routine maintenance.

| | |
---
| [](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0M2K6yAYxvGrMbsGNUaThYtzKHbVGRgKHWYT3uhrK2OSYsxH737o3MLsfzwP_K_n7gwhhvH2BgPqvPupG3GbI-aMqWP0iZDSBG6AR7bLOi-jhdFBxymvD7Q9UNY5CPHZicLpnrqWFaiZqqXiQjSyuGsH3kpqfeN7FI2zQkJjVW1dL1zrlS2Cfo3RljLGKOO0rPvWCyWV7ZEjAhBB0YU8pTLfMeMOs4_PacVU2mkoor7n_JhJ9Y9wQ7jZtq2M-61J-5rT94sQbvj1y5xO_wk31yNrPy4vWpklOFIdmRAV4XJeekaq4-XTvBdJu8cWYgyzVETQ2wAh_p7lvyVaNf8JAAD__65FeLE) | |
# Monday, September 1, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1917, Houston attorneys William Ashton Vinson and James A. Elkins established what would become one of the [world's largest law firms](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0EGSqyAQgOHT4E6r6aDEBYu3cZe3naXVQidSQUgB0Xj7qZkrzAX-v-r7us038sHHx3_a2NTPPc2RjxK4Vs6zhJMp50Ruo1e17728o6XoaEbAvoWxBTk78uGcVePMAm6UDRup-0GjUtehWQ32bFkTjrCA06w1gINBXaUcNOHFNt78xGAEKSVIhK5fxrvSg7YLIzORUMDO15S7unLlD5V7ONPOubNpa4JZa30VcfkncBI4HcfR1bJSisFH7lJ-CJxWim5J6Slw4liz5yJw2n0sKbYUXcvh6WNpsnGvw4fgy6CFgsdGPvxO6t9odoPfAQAA__9WqHkO). The firm specializes in the energy industry and has more than 700 lawyers worldwide. This week's Monday and Tuesday editions are slightly shorter than normal to give our staff a little extra time with their families over the Labor Day weekend. We'll be back with a full edition Wednesday. Texas has more than [314,600 miles](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGSqyAUgOGnwS4OnKBcCorb2GXbLZ2jHAwzKA4cTXz7nc0rbP_PV_zfj_GBMcVt-cKVHL9DHjd61UTMVEYlL8JSMvoVd56Psx7bjJvHESR0N2lvUo0eY7pG3Xg3SW9VQ06Zrjeg9b--ebopwN3ooCwYqRQSmamHoEJvO91ZxCa6X0xaqZSSCmTbTTZo05t5IiBCFFqSj5xLy09iemMN6conlXbOa5Pck3mv4v5fwCBgWPLZfqJ2yaeAYTpq3KhWAcOOCwkY4hYKVi7HzEehpji_v2JKsfZGaLmsGNNH5r_9OB38BAAA__9TSnVC) of public roads. TxDOT's governing body recently adopted a $146 billion plan for transportation projects over the next decade. Check out the details in today's _Roundup_ section. Average Americans like us already know that prices on gas, groceries, rent and childcare are only going up.
But a new jobs report underscores just how bad the economy is [https://www.cnbc.com/2025/08/04/contentious-july-jobs-report-confirms-the-us-economy-is-slowing-sharply.html] under President Trump and Congressional conservatives who promised to get our fiscal house in order. Unemployment is up, inflation is up and morale is low.
Trump didn’t like the numbers – so he’s firing the guy who compiles the country’s labor statistics. That’s what dictators do.
The American people deserve leaders who will work to fix the economy, not ones who throw tantrums when numbers don’t go their way.
It’s been awhile since I asked you to chip in . I hope I can count on your support to help restore government to leaders who value facts, people and results. [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/joc-2025?refcode=1.3]

Hello, I hope you are having a wonderful Labor Day weekend with friends and family. Summer is over and our kids are heading back to school.
I didn't think about the meaning of Labor Day for most of my life; it was simply the last day of summer and a Monday off. But when you run for President, you start learning the meaning and history of various markers in American life.
Labor Day was originally a day of celebration championed by unions to recognize workers starting in the late 1800s. It was inaugurated as a federal holiday in 1894 after a major set of strikes - the Pullman Strike - shut down most train lines from the Midwest to the West Coast. The strikes involved 250,000 workers at their peak and resulted in the deaths of at least 30 people in riots in Chicago. Congress responded by making Labor Day a national holiday and giving all federal workers the day off.
That's why we have Labor Day today.
For decades, unions were a massive force in both the American economy and politics. At their peak in 1954, 35% of working Americans belonged to a union. The United Automobile Workers was among the most influential, backing the 1963 March on Washington, the passage of Medicare and Medicaid, and the Civil Rights Act of 1964 among other accomplishments. In 1948, Walter Reuther, the head of the UAW, survived being shot at home and had his right arm rendered useless. The next year, his brother was shot at home and lost an eye. Things got that nasty. Neither shooting was ever solved.
Today, the percentage of American workers in a union is 9.9%, and despite well-reported exceptions to the contrary, organized labor has been diminishing for decades. A few sectors are highly unionized - teachers, airline workers, truckers, healthcare workers, postal workers, screen actors, police officers, firefighters - but most are not.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a7975ff1-d65f-420f-b382-567e6c6c8617?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The vast majority of unions traditionally lined up with the Democratic Party as a way to influence policy. But their rank-and-file members have increasingly broken with leadership in terms of their individual votes due to cultural polarization; it's tough to get a bunch of roughneck truckers to vote for Democrats who have taken on more and more of a coastal educated vibe, and a lot of union members like Trump. Just this month, the Teamsters [started](https://substack.com/redirect/abe51143-0574-4396-92c2-c0ecd661afee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) backing Republicans in swing districts, and it's likely that other unions will follow suit. In a time of weakening institutions, it's been hard for labor leaders to hold the line.
I posted on Twitter a few years ago, "Labor got 1 day, Capital got the other 364." That seems about right. What happens when our labor becomes more and more irrelevant in an age of superintelligent machines?
Automation was one of the big points of contention in both the Hollywood writers' strike and the dockworkers strike last year. 44% of American jobs are either repetitive cognitive or repetitive manual jobs and are thus potentially subject to automation. I talked to an insider at a major financial institution. He estimated that half of the bank's workers would be gone in the next 3 years because of AI. The vast majority of workers do not have unions representing them. Of the 4 most numerous jobs that AI is set to [replace](https://substack.com/redirect/ba046895-4214-4b2d-ab84-86215389318a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) - Customer service representatives, sales reps, marketing analysts and business analysts - none of them are unionized.
Who could be the Walter Reuther of today? Reuther put the UAW behind other causes that he saw as pro-worker. He helped found Earth Day as an example. But the footprint of labor has shrunken considerably and they will be hard-pressed to maintain the living standards and benefits of their membership, much less push for broader measures.
I spoke to a major tech entrepreneur last week. He said to me, "I fear AI will be the knockout blow to human labor." I think he's right. It's been a long time coming. Our time and energy will become more and more marginal in this new era.
Perhaps the most effective response would be a movement of people advocating for a better way of life across sectors, classes and party lines. Could that come together? And what would it take to activate such a movement? Those are the next-generation labor questions of our time.
To see what Forward is doing in your area, click [here](https://substack.com/redirect/eea186f0-236e-41a5-a852-f1f236effcee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). For me answering listener questions with Zach Graumann, click [here](https://substack.com/redirect/bbea9f09-09c0-4c79-be97-ffddf8b5a51c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). I'm working on something that will hopefully get more buying power into people's hands. The next [Offline Party](https://substack.com/redirect/5cdb15b8-9693-40ea-9243-f58571718b16?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is in New York on September 18th that will double as a launch party. Remember to look up and enjoy the long weekend.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/60c60acb-45b4-4398-a6cc-a499c4b1a2e1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

clean_body: Hello, I hope you are having a wonderful Labor Day weekend with friends and family. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more | | ---|---|--- * | | | Sep 1 | | | | | | | | | | | | Hello, I hope you are having a wonderful Labor Day weekend with friends and family. Summer is over and our kids are heading back to school. I didn't think about the meaning of Labor Day for most of my life; it was simply the last day of summer and a Monday off. But when you run for President, you start learning the meaning and history of various markers in American life. Labor Day was originally a day of celebration championed by unions to recognize workers starting in the late 1800s. It was inaugurated as a federal holiday in 1894 after a major set of strikes - the Pullman Strike - shut down most train lines from the Midwest to the West Coast. The strikes involved 250,000 workers at their peak and resulted in the deaths of at least 30 people in riots in Chicago. Congress responded by making Labor Day a national holiday and giving all federal workers the day off. That's why we have Labor Day today. For decades, unions were a massive force in both the American economy and politics. At their peak in 1954, 35% of working Americans belonged to a union. The United Automobile Workers was among the most influential, backing the 1963 March on Washington, the passage of Medicare and Medicaid, and the Civil Rights Act of 1964 among other accomplishments. In 1948, Walter Reuther, the head of the UAW, survived being shot at home and had his right arm rendered useless. The next year, his brother was shot at home and lost an eye. Things got that nasty. Neither shooting was ever solved. Today, the percentage of American workers in a union is 9.9%, and despite well-reported exceptions to the contrary, organized labor has been diminishing for decades. A few sectors are highly unionized - teachers, airline workers, truckers, healthcare workers, postal workers, screen actors, police officers, firefighters - but most are not. | | ---|---|--- The vast majority of unions traditionally lined up with the Democratic Party as a way to influence policy. But their rank-and-file members have increasingly broken with leadership in terms of their individual votes due to cultural polarization; it's tough to get a bunch of roughneck truckers to vote for Democrats who have taken on more and more of a coastal educated vibe, and a lot of union members like Trump. Just this month, the Teamsters backing Republicans in swing districts, and it's likely that other unions will follow suit. In a time of weakening institutions, it's been hard for labor leaders to hold the line. I posted on Twitter a few years ago, "Labor got 1 day, Capital got the other 364." That seems about right. What happens when our labor becomes more and more irrelevant in an age of superintelligent machines? Automation was one of the big points of contention in both the Hollywood writers' strike and the dockworkers strike last year. 44% of American jobs are either repetitive cognitive or repetitive manual jobs and are thus potentially subject to automation. I talked to an insider at a major financial institution. He estimated that half of the bank's workers would be gone in the next 3 years because of AI. The vast majority of workers do not have unions representing them. Of the 4 most numerous jobs that AI is set to - Customer service representatives, sales reps, marketing analysts and business analysts - none of them are unionized. Who could be the Walter Reuther of today? Reuther put the UAW behind other causes that he saw as pro-worker. He helped found Earth Day as an example. But the footprint of labor has shrunken considerably and they will be hard-pressed to maintain the living standards and benefits of their membership, much less push for broader measures. I spoke to a major tech entrepreneur last week. He said to me, "I fear AI will be the knockout blow to human labor." I think he's right. It's been a long time coming. Our time and energy will become more and more marginal in this new era. Perhaps the most effective response would be a movement of people advocating for a better way of life across sectors, classes and party lines. Could that come together? And what would it take to activate such a movement? Those are the next-generation labor questions of our time. To see what Forward is doing in your area, click . _Author 's note: there is not a silver bullet strategy to fixing the issues Democrats are facing with their message and navigating the modern media environment. While this article explores one issue in a host of them, it's not the only answer to our problems. Throughout By the Ballot, I plan on covering many of those issues that Democrats must tackle in order to take back the conversation and get back to winning. _
| [](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/983e5b0b-dd4f-4d5f-9129-c47719ff8bcd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Thanks for reading By the Ballot! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ieXRoZWJhbGxvdC5zdWJzdGFjay5jb20vYWNjb3VudCIsInAiOjE3MTkwNTc3NSwicyI6NTE1NjU2NiwiZiI6dHJ1ZSwidSI6OTA1MDExOTIsImlhdCI6MTc1NjcyODk2OSwiZXhwIjoyMDcyMzA0OTY5LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMCIsInN1YiI6ImxpbmstcmVkaXJlY3QifQ.IdsB9PzJ6M_9zPiNoMZafzWI2K5L2T3WGTgJMbNyz_U?)
> _" Words--so innocent and powerless as they are, as standing in a dictionary, how potent for good and evil they become in the hands of one who knows how to combine them._
> _\- Nathaniel Hawthorne, author, Salem_
> _" I'm tryna find the words to describe this girl without bein' disrespectful. _
> _Damn you a sexy bitch. " _
> _\- Aliaune Damala Bouga Time Puru Nacka Lu Lu Lu Badara Akon Thiam, poet, St. Louis_
> _" Like Hamlet, all about "words, words, words"_
> _Divide a whole into thirds, thirds, thirds_
> _I'm a gay sea otter_
> _I blow other dudes out of the water "_
> _\- Robert "Bo" Burnham, poet, Hamilton_
Words have been a flashpoint in Democratic, liberal, and progressive circles for decades--long before [Third Way's now-infamous list of 45 "banned words and phrases"](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/9c07d062-fd2b-4712-9752-ea400303f722?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) they claim Democrats should avoid. Like much of what Third Way has produced, it misses the point entirely.
We are not in a language crisis. We are in a **policy and communication crisis**. It doesn't matter if we polish our language if we can't deliver a clear message to people who need to hear it. It's not that Democrats are too "woke," as Rahm Emanuel and James Carville like to say--it's that we've spent so much time worrying about phrasing that we've forgotten how to relate to ordinary voters.
Meanwhile, Republicans weaponize language with ruthless efficiency. They turn our hesitation into ammunition. Every time Democrats run from a word or cede ground on its meaning, we let conservatives define us. And when your opponent gets to decide the dictionary, you've already lost the argument.
* * *
### **The "Magic Word" Fallacy**
[As Dan Pfeiffer recently wrote in ](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/06d8f569-c250-44cb-9c34-fa4fbc5c0e80?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[The Message Box](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/06d8f569-c250-44cb-9c34-fa4fbc5c0e80?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[,](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/06d8f569-c250-44cb-9c34-fa4fbc5c0e80?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Democrats are obsessed with finding the one "perfect phrase" that will unlock voter support. We've fallen for the **Magic Word Fallacy** --the belief that if we just say the right thing, people will instantly rally behind us.
But politics doesn't work like a Harry Potter spell. We live in an attention economy where nuance dies in 280 characters. Voters demand instant, soundbite answers to deeply complicated questions, and if they don't hear the _exact_ word they expect, outrage follows. Candidates panic, strategists scramble, and before long the entire conversation is about semantics instead of solutions.
Worse, Democrats internalize Twitter dogpiles as reality--even though, as we all know, "Twitter isn't real life." When online habits bleed into offline campaigns, we end up looking ridiculous, shaping our message around digital scolding rather than voter concerns.
* * *
### **Mightier than the sword?**
Most of the "forbidden" words Third Way listed aren't even in mainstream Democratic vocabulary. Average swing voters aren't debating whether a candidate said "Latinx" or "communities of care." Yet if you watch Fox News or right-wing podcasts, you'd think every Democrat talks exclusively in progressive buzzwords.
That's the point: Republicans co-opt fringe language to caricature our entire party. "Woke" once meant awareness of racial injustice in Black communities. Now, it's been twisted into a catch-all slur for anything conservatives dislike. By the time Democrats debate whether to use the word at all, we've already lost the frame.
And while Democrats tie ourselves in knots over phrasing, the Right gleefully normalizes slurs under the guise of "owning the libs." They amplify their trolls, caricature our activists, and present the loudest edge cases as the Democratic mainstream. Too often, we let them.
* * *
### **Talking on Eggshells**
The result is a politics of constant defensiveness. We run from words, terrified of being too restrictive _and_ terrified of being careless. Ordinary people--our base voters included--feel blindsided by ever-shifting vocabulary rules.
That's why internet personalities like Joe Rogan, Andrew Tate, and countless comedians score points by mocking "the language police." They tap into a real frustration: people hate feeling tricked or scolded for not knowing the "right" term of the week.
The problem isn't just word choice--it's that conservatives have blurred the line between **being corrected for harmful rhetoric** and **being attacked for innocent mistakes.** That conflation has real political consequences.
* * *
**Just Talk Like People**
Democrats have drifted from their working-class, plainspoken roots. Once the party of everyday language and everyday people, we now risk sounding like academic panels in human form. Voters don't need us to sound like walking thesauruses--they need us to sound like neighbors who get it.
That's why Governor Tim Walz's viral line--"these Republicans are just weird"--worked so well in 2024. It was simple, relatable, and _funny_. It punctured the Right's hysteria without jargon or condescension. Democrats desperately need more of that.
And it's why Pete Buttigieg often shines in hostile media environments. On Fox News or podcasts like _Flagrant_ , he doesn't use polling-tested buzzwords. He paints pictures of everyday life. One exchange captured it perfectly:
> _Buttigieg: "I want everyday life to be better."_
> _Schulz: "That's what they want too!"_
> _Buttigieg: "Yeah but importantly that 's what all the controversies are like. Like I want you to be able to get up in the morning and the first thing you do is you commute to work. And by the way if you have an EV, I want that to be affordable for you. Or if you're on Public Transit-not to get back into the subway situation-but I want you to have good public transit to get to where you're going. And then when you get to that job, I want you to be paid well. And if you're about to have a kid, I want you to know that you're going to have parental leave when you have that kid. And if you don't want to have a kid, I want you to have the right to choose if you want to have a kid, which means access to birth control and abortion and those things that give you the freedom to decide that."_
> _Schulz: "That's not bad."_
> _Buttigieg: "And if you already have a kid, when you pick them up at school, I want that school to be good, not having its funding slashed while they set fire to the Department of Education. And then when you get home, I want you to be in a neighborhood that is safe and where you can breathe the air because we didn't let them get rid of the Clean Air Act. And you don't have to think for one moment about whether the air you breathe or the water you drink is clean and clear, which actually takes a lot because it means the government has to constrain those actors who would make you unfree by polluting the air and polluting the water. And then when you go to bed, I want you to know your family is going to be fine, even if it's a family like mine, despite there being some Supreme Court justice who wants to obliterate your family because it doesn't match his interpretation of his religion. Like that's the life I want everybody to be able to live."_
> _Schulz: "Yeah."_
> _Buttigieg: "And I think we can deliver that."_
It's conversational, empathetic, and rooted in policy while still sounding human. Even skeptics conceded his point. That's the model Democrats need to replicate: **plain language with real stakes**.
### **The Real Task**
So where does that leave us? With three clear lessons:
1. **Stop chasing "magic words."** Voters respond to clarity, not consultant-approved vocab.
2. **Reclaim our narrative.** Don't let Republicans caricature our entire party through the fringes. Push back with our own priorities, framed simply.
3. **Be relatable again.** Speak like human beings. Not every statement has to read like a white paper.

Because at the end of the day, it's not words that win elections--it's whether voters believe you _get them_ and will fight for their lives. And no list of banned words is going to fix that.
## **TL;DR**
Democrats keep tying themselves in knots over "the right words" instead of focusing on clear, relatable messages that connect with voters. Groups like Third Way try to ban phrases, while Republicans weaponize language--twisting terms like "woke" into attacks and forcing Democrats to play defense.
Our obsession with semantics makes us look elitist and out of touch, while the Right dominates the narrative with simple, sticky phrases. Voters don't want academic jargon or poll-tested buzzwords--they want plain talk that explains how policy impacts their daily lives.
When Tim Walz calls Republicans "weird" or Pete Buttigieg breaks down policy in everyday terms, it resonates. Democrats should stop chasing "magic words," push back on GOP framing, and reclaim the conversation with clarity, empathy, and a human voice.

clean_body: When we get weighed down by words, we lose the meaning of our message. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? When we get weighed down by words, we lose the meaning of our message. | | | Sep 1 | | | | | | | | | | | | Author 's note: there is not a silver bullet strategy to fixing the issues Democrats are facing with their message and navigating the modern media environment. While this article explores one issue in a host of them, it's not the only answer to our problems. Throughout By the Ballot, I plan on covering many of those issues that Democrats must tackle in order to take back the conversation and get back to winning. | | ---|---|--- Thanks for reading By the Ballot! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work. ? " Words--so innocent and powerless as they are, as standing in a dictionary, how potent for good and evil they become in the hands of one who knows how to combine them. \- Nathaniel Hawthorne, author, Salem " I'm tryna find the words to describe this girl without bein' disrespectful. Damn you a sexy bitch. " \- Aliaune Damala Bouga Time Puru Nacka Lu Lu Lu Badara Akon Thiam, poet, St. Louis " Like Hamlet, all about "words, words, words" Divide a whole into thirds, thirds, thirds I'm a gay sea otter I blow other dudes out of the water " \- Robert "Bo" Burnham, poet, Hamilton Words have been a flashpoint in Democratic, liberal, and progressive circles for decades--long before they claim Democrats should avoid. Like much of what Third Way has produced, it misses the point entirely. We are not in a language crisis. We are in a policy and communication crisis. It doesn't matter if we polish our language if we can't deliver a clear message to people who need to hear it. It's not that Democrats are too "woke," as Rahm Emanuel and James Carville like to say--it's that we've spent so much time worrying about phrasing that we've forgotten how to relate to ordinary voters. Meanwhile, Republicans weaponize language with ruthless efficiency. They turn our hesitation into ammunition. Every time Democrats run from a word or cede ground on its meaning, we let conservatives define us. And when your opponent gets to decide the dictionary, you've already lost the argument. The "Magic Word" Fallacy Democrats are obsessed with finding the one "perfect phrase" that will unlock voter support. We've fallen for the Magic Word Fallacy --the belief that if we just say the right thing, people will instantly rally behind us. But politics doesn't work like a Harry Potter spell. We live in an attention economy where nuance dies in 280 characters. Voters demand instant, soundbite answers to deeply complicated questions, and if they don't hear the exact word they expect, outrage follows. Candidates panic, strategists scramble, and before long the entire conversation is about semantics instead of solutions. Worse, Democrats internalize Twitter dogpiles as reality--even though, as we all know, "Twitter isn't real life." When online habits bleed into offline campaigns, we end up looking ridiculous, shaping our message around digital scolding rather than voter concerns. Mightier than the sword? Most of the "forbidden" words Third Way listed aren't even in mainstream Democratic vocabulary. Average swing voters aren't debating whether a candidate said "Latinx" or "communities of care." Yet if you watch Fox News or right-wing podcasts, you'd think every Democrat talks exclusively in progressive buzzwords. That's the point: Republicans co-opt fringe language to caricature our entire party. "Woke" once meant awareness of racial injustice in Black communities. Now, it's been twisted into a catch-all slur for anything conservatives dislike. By the time Democrats debate whether to use the word at all, we've already lost the frame. And while Democrats tie ourselves in knots over phrasing, the Right gleefully normalizes slurs under the guise of "owning the libs." They amplify their trolls, caricature our activists, and present the loudest edge cases as the Democratic mainstream. Too often, we let them. Talking on Eggshells The result is a politics of constant defensiveness. We run from words, terrified of being too restrictive and terrified of being careless. Ordinary people--our base voters included--feel blindsided by ever-shifting vocabulary rules. That's why internet personalities like Joe Rogan, Andrew Tate, and countless comedians score points by mocking "the language police." They tap into a real frustration: people hate feeling tricked or scolded for not knowing the "right" term of the week. The problem isn't just word choice--it's that conservatives have blurred the line between being corrected for harmful rhetoric and being attacked for innocent mistakes. That conflation has real political consequences. Just Talk Like People Democrats have drifted from their working-class, plainspoken roots. Once the party of everyday language and everyday people, we now risk sounding like academic panels in human form. Voters don't need us to sound like walking thesauruses--they need us to sound like neighbors who get it. That's why Governor Tim Walz's viral line--"these Republicans are just weird"--worked so well in 2024. It was simple, relatable, and funny. It punctured the Right's hysteria without jargon or condescension. Democrats desperately need more of that. And it's why Pete Buttigieg often shines in hostile media environments. On Fox News or podcasts like Flagrant , he doesn't use polling-tested buzzwords. He paints pictures of everyday life. One exchange captured it perfectly: Buttigieg: "I want everyday life to be better." Schulz: "That's what they want too!" Buttigieg: "Yeah but importantly that 's what all the controversies are like. Like I want you to be able to get up in the morning and the first thing you do is you commute to work. And by the way if you have an EV, I want that to be affordable for you. Or if you're on Public Transit-not to get back into the subway situation-but I want you to have good public transit to get to where you're going. And then when you get to that job, I want you to be paid well. And if you're about to have a kid, I want you to know that you're going to have parental leave when you have that kid. And if you don't want to have a kid, I want you to have the right to choose if you want to have a kid, which means access to birth control and abortion and those things that give you the freedom to decide that." Schulz: "That's not bad." Buttigieg: "And if you already have a kid, when you pick them up at school, I want that school to be good, not having its funding slashed while they set fire to the Department of Education. And then when you get home, I want you to be in a neighborhood that is safe and where you can breathe the air because we didn't let them get rid of the Clean Air Act. And you don't have to think for one moment about whether the air you breathe or the water you drink is clean and clear, which actually takes a lot because it means the government has to constrain those actors who would make you unfree by polluting the air and polluting the water. And then when you go to bed, I want you to know your family is going to be fine, even if it's a family like mine, despite there being some Supreme Court justice who wants to obliterate your family because it doesn't match his interpretation of his religion. Like that's the life I want everybody to be able to live." Schulz: "Yeah." Buttigieg: "And I think we can deliver that." It's conversational, empathetic, and rooted in policy while still sounding human. Even skeptics conceded his point. That's the model Democrats need to replicate: plain language with real stakes. The Real Task So where does that leave us? With three clear lessons: 1. Stop chasing "magic words." Voters respond to clarity, not consultant-approved vocab. 2. Reclaim our narrative. Don't let Republicans caricature our entire party through the fringes. Push back with our own priorities, framed simply. 3. Be relatable again. Speak like human beings. Not every statement has to read like a white paper. Because at the end of the day, it's not words that win elections--it's whether voters believe you get them and will fight for their lives. And no list of banned words is going to fix that. TL;DR Democrats keep tying themselves in knots over "the right words" instead of focusing on clear, relatable messages that connect with voters. Groups like Third Way try to ban phrases, while Republicans weaponize language--twisting terms like "woke" into attacks and forcing Democrats to play defense. Our obsession with semantics makes us look elitist and out of touch, while the Right dominates the narrative with simple, sticky phrases. Voters don't want academic jargon or poll-tested buzzwords--they want plain talk that explains how policy impacts their daily lives. When Tim Walz calls Republicans "weird" or Pete Buttigieg breaks down policy in everyday terms, it resonates. Democrats should stop chasing "magic words," push back on GOP framing, and reclaim the conversation with clarity, empathy, and a human voice. Thanks for reading By the Ballot! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work. ? By the Ballot is an opinion series published on Substack. All views expressed are solely those of the author and should not be interpreted as reporting or objective journalism or attributed to any other individual or organization. I am not a journalist or reporter, nor do I claim to be one. This publication represents personal commentary, analysis, and opinion only. What is By the Ballot? By the Ballot is a free SubStack that features unapologetic dispatches from the New South 's next generation of Democrats. Commentary on stances, tactics, and news on the Left. We don't claim to know everything or always be right, we just want to be a part of the conversation. While there 's many different ways to power and the Right wants to take over our country by hook or by crook, we want to help Democrats help Americans by making a government for the people by the ballot. name: Chris Pappas
subject: Let’s send a message this Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
body:
Today is about honoring the workers who built this country.
# [](https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1&akid=2102%2E582294%2E6P9NeK)
Labor Day isn’t just about barbecues and the final days of summer. **It’s about honoring the workers who built this country and the unions that fought for fair wages, safe workplaces, and the 40-hour work week.**
It took generations to get here — and today, unions are still on the frontlines fighting for better pay, safer conditions, and a stronger middle class.
We have a president who has proven his anti-worker record time and time again, _and now he’s trying to decide who takes this Senate seat._
We can’t let one of his MAGA puppets win this race and threaten the progress that generations of workers have fought for.
In Congress I’ve stood for Granite Staters' right to join a union, earn a living wage, and work in safe and fair conditions — and I’ll do the same in the Senate.
We need to show up in droves and send a message they can’t ignore.
[**Please, donate $5 or anything you can today to put your full support behind unions and our campaign.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_20250901_1_laborday?refcode=em20250901-2102&amount=5&refcode2=2102_582294_6P9NeK&akid=2102%2E582294%2E6P9NeK)
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_20250901_1_laborday?amount=5&refcode=em20250901-2102&refcode2=2102_582294_6P9NeK&akid=2102%2E582294%2E6P9NeK)**
Thank you,
Chris Pappas
**New Hampshire is poised to be one of the closest Senate races in the country in 2026. Will you chip in today to help Chris stop the Republicans from flipping the Granite State red and expanding their Senate majority?**
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_footer&refcode=em20250901-1?t=2&refcode2=2102_582294_6P9NeK&akid=2102%2E582294%2E6P9NeK)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.chrispappas.org/signup/cps_receive_fewer_emails/?t=3&akid=2102%2E582294%2E6P9NeK)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.chrispappas.org/signup/cps_update_your_information/?t=4&akid=2102%2E582294%2E6P9NeK)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.chrispappas.org/unsubscribe/cps_unsubscribe/?t=5&akid=2102%2E582294%2E6P9NeK)

Email us: [teampappas@chrispappas.org](mailto:teampappas@chrispappas.org)

Chris Pappas for Senate
PO Box 313
Manchester, NH 03105

| | | | **Peter,** They’ve tried to silence us. They’ve tried to ignore us. But we’re still here — and we’re not going anywhere. As we approach the September 30th fundraising deadline, we’re setting an ambitious goal: **$20,000 to close out this quarter strong.** This isn’t just about dollars — it’s about **defiance**. It’s about proving that Tennessee Democrats are united, unyielding, and ready to take on a supermajority that’s putting billionaires and backroom deals ahead of working families.
---
| | [ Help Us Hit Our Goal! ](https://ctrk.klclick1.com/l/01K42N7A4DT579D4389G6096YA_2)
---
| Let’s be clear:
🗳️ They want to gerrymander our districts to keep your vote from counting.
🏛️ They’re attacking our local sovereignty to silence cities like Nashville.
🚧 And they’re greenlighting billion-dollar boondoggles like underground tunnels for tech billionaires while rural hospitals close and schools go underfunded. This quarter is a gut-check moment. **Will we let them steamroll us? Or will we stand together and say, “Enough”?** ** ** 🚨**Chip in now and help us hit our $20,000 goal by September 30th.** Every dollar goes directly to organizing, outreach, and electing Democrats who will put Tennesseans first — not the wealthy few.**
**

Tennessee House Democratic Caucus

name: Team NPP
subject: Happy Labor Day from Team NPP!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New Pennsylvania Project
body:
| | | [](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9Yr19exo8ATdRfiqRDml-2FPz7Gzt7xGALSZRspEZtT5xEnKXo3V0RTAgc0eUuqqD5aYeuqrdu4QrzhSgH4TofrEar1VPRnN-2BIf8rgHdAVHvR57YMkyf-2BSnreKr6Nk1h494dw-3D-3Dimq4_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYeOX-2FEZVHHcV1reEV-2BA-2BUbGJNdMpZOZedavr9mmECwI55DDrMOHRGPjj-2BjEY14Dm4GSu4v4B8HYVhliF0U2GRUmRu4CY-2BnqNpGHbWlgL7kAkW-2BqJ1uaPPTJ-2BUj1IZwAllaheuWEKxgbWyBS-2FovDy9lEccO61kzJZ36sAQHelc11xgsPzINHlXMeuczNop8AP5)
---
Fellow activist, We wanted to reach out to thank you for your support as we end the Summer and, most importantly, to wish you a happy Labor Day!

The labor movement harnessed collective power to transform our nation, built the middle class, and won critical worker protections — despite often violent and sometimes deadly resistance. Every day, we are inspired by the labor movement’s tireless efforts and sacrifices.

Similarly, expanding the electorate is about using collective power to accomplish transformative change.

**That’s why we are out every day in our centered communities across Pennsylvania, engaging Pennsylvanians to tap into their power by voting in every single election to shape our state for the better. **

We still have a lot of work to do to improve worker conditions in Pennsylvania – including continuing the fight to increase the minimum wage, which has been stuck at $7.25 an hour for more than a decade. That is why it’s important to elect candidates who represent the change and policies we all want to see for our future. And that is why it’s so important that we continue to engage eligible voters to turn out for every election, twice a year.

**Thank you for standing with us in this fight today and every day.**

In solidarity,

Team NPP
---
_Donations to the New Pennsylvania Project supports our staff’s community engagement efforts including; organizing and hosting town halls, educational webinars, community conversations, and creating digital content, bi-weekly blasts and social media to reach the communities we engage year-round.
_
---
| | _The New Pennsylvania Project (NPP) is a voting rights organization with a year-round primary focus on voter registration, civic education, and mobilization. NPP centers historically disenfranchised and often neglected Black, Indigenous and other people of color, immigrant communities and the youth in our work. Through civic engagement, we ensure all eligible voters feel compelled to exercise their freedom to vote in the Commonwealth._

clean_body: | | | Fellow activist, We wanted to reach out to thank you for your support as we end the Summer and, most importantly, to wish you a happy Labor Day! The labor movement harnessed collective power to transform our nation, built the middle class, and won critical worker protections — despite often violent and sometimes deadly resistance. Every day, we are inspired by the labor movement’s tireless efforts and sacrifices. Similarly, expanding the electorate is about using collective power to accomplish transformative change. That’s why we are out every day in our centered communities across Pennsylvania, engaging Pennsylvanians to tap into their power by voting in every single election to shape our state for the better. We still have a lot of work to do to improve worker conditions in Pennsylvania – including continuing the fight to increase the minimum wage, which has been stuck at $7.25 an hour for more than a decade. That is why it’s important to elect candidates who represent the change and policies we all want to see for our future. And that is why it’s so important that we continue to engage eligible voters to turn out for every election, twice a year. Thank you for standing with us in this fight today and every day. In solidarity, Team NPP Donations to the New Pennsylvania Project supports our staff’s community engagement efforts including; organizing and hosting town halls, educational webinars, community conversations, and creating digital content, bi-weekly blasts and social media to reach the communities we engage year-round. | | The New Pennsylvania Project (NPP) is a voting rights organization with a year-round primary focus on voter registration, civic education, and mobilization. NPP centers historically disenfranchised and often neglected Black, Indigenous and other people of color, immigrant communities and the youth in our work. Through civic engagement, we ensure all eligible voters feel compelled to exercise their freedom to vote in the Commonwealth. Will you support us with a monthly contribution today? || || || The New Pennsylvania Project is a 501(c)(4). Contributions are not for charitable purposes and are not tax deductible. | Paid for by the New Pennsylvania Project Our Contact Information New Pennsylvania ProjectP.O. Box 443 West Chester, PA 19381412-699-6771 | | | |
