In many ways, we would become a Third World Nation..." | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? "If a Radical Left Court is allowed to terminate these Tariffs, almost all of this investment, and much more, will be immediately cancelled! In many ways, we would become a Third World Nation..." Sep 1 | | | | | | | | | | | Hello Capitalists, Here 's everything you need to be following today: Trump is on the warpath after Appeals Court throws out his tariffs Gas prices plummet over Labor Day to 5 year lows Alibaba soars on the back of homemade chips and AI EU Chief's plane has its GPS jammed over Bulgaria Today 's markets: DOW: ⛔️ CLOSED ⛔️ S &P: ⛔️ CLOSED ⛔️ NASDAQ: ⛔️ CLOSED ⛔️ CBOE VIX Volatility Index: ⛔️ CLOSED ⛔️ Trump issues dire warning after tariffs get thrown out by Federal Appeals court President Donald Trump warned Sunday that a federal court's ruling against his tariffs could cripple $15 trillion in U.S. investment, potentially plunging the nation into escalating trade policy tensions. | | ---|---|--- Court Rules Tariffs Unlawful : A federal appeals court declared most of Trump's tariffs illegal under the IEEPA (International Emergency Economic Powers Act,) affirming a prior U.S. Court of International Trade ruling. Tariffs Remain Temporarily Active : The tariffs stay in effect until October 14 creating uncertainty for U.S. trade policy. Trump wrote: "If a Radical Left Court is allowed to terminate these Tariffs, almost all of this investment, and much more, will be immediately cancelled! In many ways, we would become a Third World Nation, with no hope of GREATNESS again. TIME IS OF THE ESSENCE!!! " | | ---|---|--- White House trade counselor Peter Navarro against Trump's tariffs as "weaponized partisan injustice," signaling a Supreme Court appeal to defend U.S. trade protections. Navarro Alleges Democratic Bias: Navarro claimed the appeals court's decision was driven by Democratic appointees, with six of seven judges and 12 blue states opposing Trump's tariff policies. Strong Dissent Offers Hope: A robust dissent in the 7-4 ruling provides a roadmap for a Supreme Court appeal, addressing issues like national emergency validity and tariff permanence. | | ---|---|--- No, it's not Nvidia... It's Mode Mobile , 2023's fastest-growing software company according to Deloitte. Their EarnPhone has helped users earn and save over $325M, driving $75M+ in revenue and . And having secured partnerships with Walmart and Best Buy , Mode's not stopping there... Like Uber turned vehicles into income-generating assets, Mode is turning smartphones into . The difference is that you still have a chance to invest in Mode's pre-IPO offering before the window closes. They've just been granted the stock ticker $MODE by the Nasdaq and over 50,000 investors participated in their previously sold out offering at $0.30/share. ⏳ Gas prices hit 5 year low as "all-of-the-above" energy policy starts to pay off Gas prices plummeted to a for Labor Day 2025, as Trump's energy policies unleashed American production, easing costs for millions of holiday travelers nationwide. | | ---|---|--- Energy Dominance Drives Savings : Trump's reversal of Biden-era green policies boosted U.S. energy output, directly lowering fuel costs and benefiting consumers, per White House statements. Gas Prices Trend Downward : Analysts predict prices may drop below $3 per gallon in September 2025 as demand falls post-holiday, offering further relief. Policy Shift Fuels Prosperity : Energy Secretary Chris Wright credits Trump's "all-of-the-above" energy strategy for creating jobs and enhancing economic and national security. Comparative Price Decline : Gas prices fell from $3.29 in 2024 and $3.77 in 2022, showcasing significant progress under Trump's energy agenda. Alibaba shares soar on the back of AI growth and chip development Alibaba's Hong Kong shares as its cloud unit's AI-driven growth and a new AI chip development fueled investor optimism in China's tech giant. | | ---|---|--- Cloud Unit Accelerates Growth: Alibaba's cloud computing division reported strong quarterly results, driven by surging demand for AI-related products, boosting overall revenue despite a broader revenue miss. New AI Chip Development: Sources revealed Alibaba is developing a new AI-focused chip, aiming to strengthen domestic semiconductor capabilities and enhance its technological edge in AI applications. Instant Commerce Investment Surge: Alibaba is heavily investing in China's competitive "instant commerce" market, though this contributed to an 18% drop in net income excluding investment gains. Investor Confidence Rebounds Strongly: The stock rally reflects growing investor support for Alibaba's strategic shift toward AI and cloud infrastructure, building on a 13% U.S. share increase Friday. EU commission confirms "GPS interference" to chief's plane mid-air over Bulgaria Suspected Russian carrying EU chief Ursula von der Leyen over Bulgaria on Sunday, forcing pilots to navigate manually, escalating tensions as she tours frontline nations. | | ---|---|--- Bulgarian Authorities Suspect Russia: The European Commission confirmed GPS interference on von der Leyen's plane, with Bulgarian officials pointing to Russia as the likely culprit behind the deliberate disruption. Safe Landing Despite Jamming: The chartered plane landed safely in Plovdiv using paper maps, allowing von der Leyen to continue her tour of EU states bordering Russia and Belarus. EU Strengthens Defense Resolve: The incident reinforces the EU's commitment to bolster defense capabilities and support Ukraine, amid ongoing Russian hybrid threats like GPS jamming. Kremlin Denies Involvement: Russian spokesperson Dmitry Peskov called the jamming allegations "incorrect," while the EU noted a rise in such incidents since the Ukraine war began. India seeks to strengthen ties with China and Japan in the face of US tariffs Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi arrived in China Sunday, seeking to mend ties strained by border disputes, as , signaling a potential economic realignment against Washington's pressure. | | ---|---|--- Border Dispute Progress : India and China are cautiously de-escalating tensions from the 2020 Galwan Valley clash, resuming direct flights and discussing visa relaxations to bolster economic ties. SCO's Strategic Role : Modi's attendance at the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation summit highlights India's renewed interest in the China-Russia-led bloc, driven by strained U.S. relations and tariff disputes. Economic Cooperation Potential : India aims to leverage China's manufacturing strength for electronics production, seeking lower import duties to reduce its $99.2 billion trade deficit with Beijing. Regional Alliance Shifts : Modi's Japan visit en route to China underscores India's push for diversified partnerships and closer ties for regional supply chain stability. body:
The rumors have been confirmed. Obama made the call.
| Friend,
---
Rumors were circulating that Obama was meddling behind the curtain in the New York City Mayor’s race in support of Zohran Mamdani – a communist who wants to abolish prisons and private property.
---
_Those rumors have now been confirmed by Mamdani himself._
---
**Obama called him to congratulate him and guide him to victory in an election nearly 2 months away.**
---
_In fact, the entire Obama Network has been quietly meeting with Mamdani._
---
Why? _Because they see Zohran Mamdani as the next Barack Obama_.
---
You may be inclined to brush this off as a problem for New York City alone...
---
_**...But knowing what you know now, would you have brushed off a similar warning about a relatively unknown community organizer from Chicago named Barack Obama?**_
---
If you could go back in time and stop Obama before his rise to power, would you?
---
As the most senior elected Republican in New York, I am making a _solemn pledge_ to you right now: I will fight with every fiber of my being to stop Mamdani and FIRE Governor Kathy Hochul who singlehandedly enabled his rise to power.
---
_That’s _my_ pledge. Now I’m entrusting _you_ with a 500% MATCH that I’ve authorized to fight back as the Obama Machine tries to install a communist mayor in the biggest city in America._
---

name: Roy Cooper
subject: We could not do this without you. Thank you.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
I hope you’ll show everyone you’re with us. |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114426322/566984754/823540626?refcode=ea.cbeae632-5a87-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ms=ea.cbeae632-5a87-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYmVhZTYzMi01YTg3LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xQXkfTJWtQLr2OcCgYj28k3XRUGyqt9MOZGLi-fLlTY=&emci=0d46122a-5a87-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=cbeae632-5a87-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=17205535)
Hi Peter, I’m used to close races here in North Carolina. _Each time I’ve run for office, we’ve won — even in some of North Carolina’s closest elections._ The grassroots support we have earned in this campaign and every campaign before is due to one guiding principle, and I do my best to uphold it every day: do everything possible to make life better for as many people as possible. I’ve been honored to see so much enthusiasm for our campaign for the U.S. Senate from every corner of the state and across the country. The support we’ve received has exceeded all my hopes, and there are too many people to thank — but I’d like to sincerely express my gratitude. Thank you for your faith and trust in us. Thank you for supporting us. Thank you for making the hard fights worth it. **[You’ll hear plenty more from me over the next 14 months, but _thank you_ is the most important thing I can say. And the most important request I can make of you is to show your support publicly by adding your endorsement today. name: Kim Schrier
subject: Celebrating Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress
body:
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114426383/566984902/693975070?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZDI1MjhmNS01YTg3LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=K_VGcDSwBe0ZQxgWo5-3LKprCUpAIZ7wq80tPo2GCXs=&emci=bd2ead52-e184-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=9d2528f5-5a87-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=15820338)
---

Peter,

Labor Day is more than a long weekend—**it’s a reminder of the generations of workers who organized, sacrificed, and fought for the protections we enjoy today.**

I think about my mom, who was a public school teacher and proud union member. Watching her taught me the value of solidarity and the power of collective bargaining.

She and her colleagues stood shoulder-to-shoulder to secure smaller class sizes, fair pay, and the benefits that helped sustain our family, _including the pension and health coverage my parents still count on today_. Because of the labor movement, workers across Washington and the nation have safer workplaces, stronger wages, and the ability to retire with dignity. **But the work isn’t finished.**

This Labor Day, let’s recommit ourselves to honoring working people, and to standing with unions, under extreme threat right now from this administration, as they continue the fight for fairness and opportunity.

Happy Labor Day!

—Kim Rep. Kim Schrier is a doctor, scientist, and mom, fighting for Washington's 8th District in Congress. This is one of the most competitive districts in the country, so we need your help to keep Kim in Congress. Chip in now to help protect Kim's seat. | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114426384/566984903/-2117271196?refcode=em25_250901_lday_enask_gq_act___KS_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250901_lday_enask_gq_act___KS_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=9d2528f5-5a87-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZDI1MjhmNS01YTg3LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=K_VGcDSwBe0ZQxgWo5-3LKprCUpAIZ7wq80tPo2GCXs=)

subject: They begged for it
body:
Now I want to send you one.
What it represents is more powerful than you realize

| | | [](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=1ee231193c3d4e608892f4a7b34c6fd1&_e=LqP_gFk3cGuPR3OMoviMGDaskW2hGA5J8RIM4jljxsERgw4aQVlUTA1nELOEk51-7hAFknCH5EB89r0XxzsZ0w_WdZefjNrqZcZmoTWPRKJariM8ihSTqxC8UvMekGAVWKaT6N7BTRiCp2qBSJkvqTYEp42_UiYieoyvxDgGpqxW_d-w5JfxG0O2WMUHWp3pNJecmDPixRil2epi2g6J6HeR8rfI0gpJngxoiKehDrbhmM0cIsqRipJ05bYAE1h5PTaZ0TCIE9qWgVgxmUVv0TYEvfDJ21v9j9rWNqVb9a8C56HUsKAout_8l8PxwUaqzYA3A0A6ySsvw_i7Yewufhuw2-tENXFK_EDdndp9vIKX1LTrNXw-cB6A9viorBKkPUcYHnOVLc0BYcq3D4ZoaW0aCCELG2ISLC3eGayjwXXeMeZkTb8IQkODYrrj93srpRZaIgyY0E-tVZyzB9pl9C6Ez0Ei2ZH-0vyMLkjWndLro-tno9HCaIA_GwwMs7MswcPXUB0j6HyA5UE8pI2pSg%3D%3D)
---
Peter,
---
I'll cut straight to the point... I want to send you a FREE limited-edition "47" hat...The same hat college students have literally begged for during our campus tour stops.
---
[](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=1ee231193c3d4e608892f4a7b34c6fd1&_e=LqP_gFk3cGuPR3OMoviMGDaskW2hGA5J8RIM4jljxsERgw4aQVlUTA1nELOEk51-7hAFknCH5EB89r0XxzsZ0w_WdZefjNrqZcZmoTWPRKJariM8ihSTqxC8UvMekGAVWKaT6N7BTRiCp2qBSJkvqWR6CusX69wZfW5ccPdZdauzIRvfad6UMfGvfvgSlNRdARtqxAL07ZAVw0x9qRSHQbVMI6WBkC8wBV9X3NCgMj88iobAUo0I_CEv6qNAKOFcCC1w1QoCEP1xN4R9L5GSm4PtuzEJGHEMlwq2M-TWafVZfWPTqfLTChxaK32_msMF7QTZjAk-bFIhHHzYRXLOvx5wjQIeihs3TpIt0IF3Unr1j40zC_O9kSFtJHKlsAQSbQMG8xVEocTzyF5P7Ac2MNC0yEbHej6zop2McRk3Q1n3DOGMA9AnUp6XuNq-qL-jsbtcdn3wGzIFEVKGE-hXtuwblQpZUANyj4G_QGZ7KR7e36cEFKJdK9d49Kr6wkVSsY7yqvTHJz9YCuVJmGyyGQ%3D%3D)
---
But this isn't just any hat... It's a symbol.A symbol of what's happening right now across college campuses in America. _Students are waking up... Chanting "USA!"... Waving flags... and standing up for faith, family, and freedom._Everywhere Turning Point USA goes, we're seeing record crowds -- thousands of students -- fired up to defend this country and reject the Left's lies.And guess what?**They 're PROUD to be American.** This isn't just a moment... It's not a mere "shift"... This is a generational REVOLUTION!**And Peter, I want YOU to be part of it.**_All I ask?_**Make a gift of ANY amount to help Turning Point USA reach even more young people ...**...and I'll send you a limited-edition "47" hat as my thank-you.

| | | | | Trump is devastating our democracy, and we need a strong wave of support to show that opposition to his presidency is stronger than ever. Speak Up PAC is working to elect Democrats up and down the ballot to resist Republican extremism. But unfortunately, this month has been very tough for fundraising for Speak Up PAC and our friends at Blue Amp Action. Now is a critical time for this movement, and I’m counting on you to step up, Peter. [So please, will you chip in $5 or anything you can before our midnight deadline to support Speak Up PAC and Blue Amp Action?](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tefbf800c-fb42-422d-8d09-ff06f84a94be/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Te175786e-2955-41b2-aae0-3dd84fc16076/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: Mazie Hirono
subject: I wouldn’t have it any other way
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mazie Hirono For Hawaii**
body:
|
#
---
Happy Labor Day, Peter! Today, I’m reminded of the rich and proud tradition of organized labor in Hawaii and thankful for the brave workers who have made enormous sacrifices to advance workers’ rights in our country. The labor movement has played a crucial role in securing the protections we enjoy as workers — from livable wages and safer working conditions to child labor laws and the eight-hour workday. As the GOP continues its all-out assault on labor, and corporate greed continues to be rewarded, we must continue working for fair pay, decent treatment, and basic dignity. That’s why I’m grateful that grassroots people like you helped us close the month of August out strong. Your support is fueling our fight to defend working families from the GOP’s billionaire-first agenda. I know we reached out a lot asking for your support, and I want to emphasize that it really does make a difference. My campaign has always been powered by grassroots supporters like you, and I wouldn’t have it any other way. Thank you for standing with me, Peter. — Mazie | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114427639/566989283/1135625285?refcode=AU_MKH_FR_GEN_TYE_AC_20250901_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MTU3NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNTcxYTlhNy02MTg3LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NmPGJGmWFXR7fF6-U1OWHWK_NW0ADGKowBx0sf9dQk0=)

Hi Peter, it’s Jonathan Jackson.

This afternoon, I received a HUGE update from my team, and I knew I had to share it with you:

From the genocide in Gaza to Trump's attacks on democracy in America, organized labor can help restore the rule of law--and needs your solidarity today

# [Organized labor is the key to overcoming the cruelty of capital](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=172506119&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcyNTA2MTE5LCJpYXQiOjE3NTY3NTE2NTgsImV4cCI6MTc1OTM0MzY1OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.zP6JFERvo4fPJRStbN_8spzr0NfhYuXSDnwjvng6N6o)
### From the genocide in Gaza to Trump's attacks on democracy in America, organized labor can help restore the rule of law--and needs your solidarity today
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)

While I enjoy holidays as much as anyone, I often [chafe](https://substack.com/redirect/40ee121e-600e-4366-9ced-fafbf3ea4872?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) at the [reduction](https://substack.com/redirect/45af3dc7-0686-43f5-bea5-daa3fce77c36?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of crucial [principles](https://substack.com/redirect/8ce56445-6cd9-426d-8cdf-d7c8f9a4aeb0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) worthy of commemoration to mere excuses to celebrate. Today's Labor Day holiday represents a quintessence of that pattern, as it has shifted in the popular consciousness from a celebration of organized labor to a simple calendar marker indicating the end of summer.
But labor is a lot more than an excuse to celebrate. It was long the crucial bulwark constraining the worst vagaries of capital, and had a storied history in the United States--until the Democratic Party [turned its back on unions](https://substack.com/redirect/ba544be5-5fde-46b4-9361-1c2e7bdb3d33?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) while absurdly and outrageously continuing to enjoy the sector's support.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f9dd7493-59d9-45d8-b17f-fc1c3cf268a5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Today, organized labor could offer a potential key to challenging the excesses of the Trump administration, and also ending the ongoing Israeli genocide in Gaza.
_Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! You can help inform --and inspire--your friends by sharing this post._
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=172506119&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcyNTA2MTE5LCJpYXQiOjE3NTY3NTE2NTgsImV4cCI6MTc1OTM0MzY1OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.zP6JFERvo4fPJRStbN_8spzr0NfhYuXSDnwjvng6N6o)
### **Labor vs. militarism**
More so than any other sector of civil society, organized labor could end the genocide in Gaza.
Beyond [championing transformative expansions of labor law](https://substack.com/redirect/a9937e4a-23c4-4b2f-b2a8-3025d4e53afc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as a congressional candidate, I've written three posts on this theme. One came in the immediate wake of the genocide's escalation in late 2023. I wrote the second and third this summer, in the wake of U.S. labor leader Chris Smalls being detained and beaten by Israeli authorities in retaliation for showing solidarity with human rights principles.
The first reviewed a series of labor leaders who have recently confronted capital and emerged victorious. They include Chris Smalls from the Amazon Labor Union, Sara Nelson from the Association of Flight Attendants, and Shawn Fain from United Auto Workers (not to be confused with [Sean O'Brien](https://substack.com/redirect/64053f80-23e6-45b8-b05b-54acdd486a3e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from the Teamsters).
## [We, the People, can unplug the war machine](https://substack.com/redirect/4036d9f8-bbce-4d25-94fa-e89a56495d22?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/profile/15484941-shahid-buttar)| | *
---|---|---
| November 2, 2023
[](https://substack.com/redirect/4036d9f8-bbce-4d25-94fa-e89a56495d22?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[The world has watched in horror as a day of mass trauma for Isreal has escalated into an outright genocide of Palestinians living in Gaza. People around the world have taken to the streets by the millions in a global movement unrivaled by any since we tried to stop the American invasion of Iraq 20 years ago.](https://substack.com/redirect/4036d9f8-bbce-4d25-94fa-e89a56495d22?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/4036d9f8-bbce-4d25-94fa-e89a56495d22?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
It concludes:
> Few figures are better poised than the three of them to lead the labor movement to a new era of solidarity. They are uniquely poised to invite other sectors of organized labor to walk off the job until Washington sees fit to finally respect international human rights by joining the rest of the world that has repeatedly called for cease-fires in Gaza that Washington has effectively blocked.
The second post in that series observed Israel's detention of Chris Smalls, and its discriminatory state violence responding to his leadership and demonstrations of solidarity.
## [The empire strikes back](https://substack.com/redirect/dfa668b3-0b8d-4eed-b3a8-231088fc86e4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/profile/15484941-shahid-buttar)| | *
---|---|---
| Jul 30
[](https://substack.com/redirect/dfa668b3-0b8d-4eed-b3a8-231088fc86e4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[I often give thanks, for whatever it's work, for some prescience in the course of unfolding current events. For example, the continuing consolidation of authoritarianism in America offers that opportunity on a seemingly daily basis: I spent a decade advocating for greater restraints on executive power, while Republicans and Democrats](https://substack.com/redirect/dfa668b3-0b8d-4eed-b3a8-231088fc86e4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

It observed how:
> His work challenging Jeff Bezos made Smalls a global labor hero. Coming from someone with such well-deserved respect across the labor movement, his recognition of solidarity of Gaza--and outspoken advocacy on behalf of human rights in Palestine--seemed like a gateway through which a broader constituency (both within and beyond the U.S.) might come to care about ending the genocide in Gaza.
> Alongside the corruption of Congress, the courts, and the White House, militarism also relies on the participation of labor. Should an intersectionally resurgent labor movement assertively challenge genocide--by, for instance, maintaining strikes impeding any of a series of critical industries like transportation or shipping--Washington could be forced to capitulate to popular demands.
The third article reported on grassroots mobilizations around the world supporting human rights in Gaza, and Chris' return to the United States after his release by Israeli authorities.
## [Return of the Jedi](https://substack.com/redirect/7479e620-1b02-4a64-9546-bf613b17b696?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

[](https://substack.com/redirect/7479e620-1b02-4a64-9546-bf613b17b696?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[I'm writing today to celebrate an important victory for the grassroots movement challenging the genocide in Gaza. Amid other glimmers of hope emerging as the Israeli starvation campaign continues to horrify the world, it offers an indication of growing support for peace and human rights in a critical sector poised to force human rights on Washington, despite the corruption of the two corporate political parties and their consensus favoring genocide.](https://substack.com/redirect/7479e620-1b02-4a64-9546-bf613b17b696?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

It reflected on "glimmers of hope emerging as the Israeli starvation campaign continues to horrify the world," including grassroots mobilizations around the world. Along with Chris' experience, they:
> offer[] an indication of growing support for peace and human rights in a critical sector poised to force human rights on Washington, despite the corruption of the two corporate political parties and their consensus favoring genocide.
Labor is a sleeping giant, and perhaps the only force that can stop the rise of the right wing and the genocide in Gaza it has enabled.

### **Capital vs. labor**
As the Trump administration continues to escalate its war on America, its economy, and the Constitution, organized labor has also felt the impact.
Unfortunately, despite--or perhaps precisely due to--labor's overwhelming potential influence, it was among the first sectors hamstrung by the Trump administration.
Most observers have already grown to lament the administration's firing of [over a quarter million public sector employees](https://substack.com/redirect/46402f97-75e8-4984-9af5-a92c3530b207?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Government employees, meanwhile, have been among the most heavily unionized workforces in the country, since (until recently) unionbusting was more the province of private employers than the federal and state governments.
Among Trump's very first acts upon returning to office in 2025 was the illegal dismissal of Gwynne Wilcox, a member of the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) whose racial identity may have made her an [especially easy target](https://substack.com/redirect/f54511f5-1eea-4ce1-9db1-e031e45564d1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for the president.
The legal saga sparked by Wilcox's dismissal remains ongoing. A U.S. district court [attempted to reinstate](https://substack.com/redirect/94d9298c-3bdc-4202-8d84-a6fd959b1554?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) her in early March, only to be [overruled](https://substack.com/redirect/c2348a0b-87ca-49a6-85a1-b7a114a90f3d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by a three-judge appellate panel later that month. They were, in turn, [overruled](https://substack.com/redirect/e9e77d9f-17e9-4129-a1a4-3252ae3b2ba1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by the U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C. Circuit sitting _en banc_ (meaning that all the judges on the court were invited to participate, rather than just the three judges who were randomly assigned at an earlier stage in the process). Within two days, Chief Justice John Roberts [stayed](https://substack.com/redirect/d243cf4a-f008-44c4-afb7-b36ebedf691d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that decision pending an ongoing appeal to the [weaponized and partisan U.S. Supreme Court](https://substack.com/redirect/eb3f0bfb-c954-4d50-b2c8-9210e06ee1af?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that he oversees.
Most of the news coverage surrounding Wilcox's firing, and the effort to reinstate her, has focused on the issue of whether the executive branch wields the power to dismiss members of independent agencies created by Congress. While the constitutional separation of powers is among [my greatest and most longstanding concerns](https://substack.com/redirect/04887f66-e3b9-4b3a-8559-33862fd931fb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), I aim here to focus not on the doctrinal aspect of these cases, but the impact on the ground of Wilcox being denied the chance to complete her appointed term on the NLRB.
Whatever vestiges of labor rights that workers in the U.S. theoretically enjoy today will effectively remain in exile as long as the NLRB lacks a quorum. Put simply, there is nowhere for workers to bring complaints about violations of their collective rights by predatory employers.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=172506119&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcyNTA2MTE5LCJpYXQiOjE3NTY3NTE2NTgsImV4cCI6MTc1OTM0MzY1OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.zP6JFERvo4fPJRStbN_8spzr0NfhYuXSDnwjvng6N6o)
Removing Wilcox from the NLRB basically represented union busting at a structural scale, impeding new unions from forming in the first place and hanging workers out to dry. At the same time, the right wing has also mounted two parallel assaults on labor: Trump has nominated union busting figures to fill Wilcox's seat on the NLRB, while business leaders are challenging its constitutionality before [our co-opted courts](https://substack.com/redirect/83bf6ff7-a137-4eab-9772-e5cdc43b33a3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
As explained by [a fellow writer on this platform](https://substack.com/redirect/659d8071-c0ba-4697-b6d3-85a40153da24?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> The NLRB hears and settles disputes between workers and employers, but Wilcox's departure deprived it of a quorum, making it unable to hear cases. Effectively, employers [are] free to violate already-weak labor law at will knowing there [is] no NLRB to penalize them.
> Trump has since tapped two nominees to staff the Board and return its function, but that might be worse in the long run. His first nominee is the former chief counsel to Trump's appointed NLRB Chairman, and the other built his career at the notorious union-busting firm Morgan, Lewis & Bockius LLP.
> These appointments might not even matter, as Trump's favorite billionaires have advanced a lawsuit through the hard right 5th Circuit court arguing the NLRB is unconstitutional. The case is on its way to Republican-controlled Supreme Court, where our unelected Harry Potter impersonators might complete conservatism's near-century-long campaign to scrap the New Deal.
I'll leave aside for now the profound frustration raised by workers who supported Trump [to their own detriment](https://substack.com/redirect/792e2caa-2b0d-43b9-bacf-d101c7a1da40?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). We could, after all, discuss the president's [various enablers](https://substack.com/redirect/4f18a59f-e3eb-469f-9285-ac43144393f6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) until the end of time.
For now, the point is that recent attacks on the right of organize have bloodied a sector that was already on its heels, hollowed out by longstanding trends in the economy including the corporate outsourcing that drove so much manufacturing abroad (and into prisons, where Americans by the millions [continue to work as slaves](https://substack.com/redirect/aaa42034-7441-48f6-abc6-bd0af9978b40?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) 160 years after the Union victory in the Civil War).

[](https://substack.com/redirect/917a9915-257b-46b3-8188-a5eb022a52ca?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
A post shared by [@jacobinmag](https://substack.com/redirect/2abc6b24-5c18-45c5-8f12-33dc402c3612?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

### **Show solidarity today --and build your local network**
Today, Americans are [mobilizing](https://substack.com/redirect/2d90edf6-3cc6-4cbf-a833-73eb1980c1be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in hundreds of communities across the country to show solidarity with labor. As [reported](https://substack.com/redirect/a172f243-259e-435c-8810-52df391c745f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by _the Guardian_ :
> Nearly 1,000 "worker over billionaire" protests are being planned in all 50 states starting this weekend as part of a Labor Day week of action organized by labor unions and advocacy groups in opposition to the Trump administration's policies.
> The actions include marches and rallies in cities such as [Chicago](https://substack.com/redirect/ad30ca72-7df7-4c6c-9d0c-daee94db63f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [Los Angeles](https://substack.com/redirect/86809c85-f956-4b7b-8ba5-191f609bdcb1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a Labor Day parade in [New York City](https://substack.com/redirect/56446670-32f7-49a1-856e-8e08226180f0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), rallies in Palmer, [Alaska](https://substack.com/redirect/a936f87a-f6ea-40b4-9cd5-48c7f1a518cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), Freeport, [Maine](https://substack.com/redirect/680fc9c8-8648-4edb-b6fa-e30f27ad3c4d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and a planned protest at the state capitol in Honolulu, [Hawaii](https://substack.com/redirect/1ea124fc-bb70-4f1b-b33d-7e06422be4e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
> The protests are organized by the AFL-CIO, the largest federation of labor unions in the US, and dozens of partner organizations, including Public Citizen, Indivisible, Democracy Forward, MoveOn and Patriotic Millionaires.
> 'This is about organic, grassroots organizing, and we intentionally wanted it to be outside of Washington DC, because that's where the impacts are being felt,' Liz Shuler, president of the AFL-CIO, said.
> 'Whether it's teachers or nurses or construction workers, they're all determined to stand up and fight back, because they're experiencing the cuts, they're experiencing the change in policies, they're experiencing the attacks of this White House on their unions, and so they're determined to make their voices heard and mobilize to fight forward regardless of what's happening around us, no matter the obstacles.'
I'll be joining a gathering in my community today, and encourage you to [do the same wherever you live](https://substack.com/redirect/a9937e4a-23c4-4b2f-b2a8-3025d4e53afc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=172506119&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcyNTA2MTE5LCJpYXQiOjE3NTY3NTE2NTgsImV4cCI6MTc1OTM0MzY1OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.zP6JFERvo4fPJRStbN_8spzr0NfhYuXSDnwjvng6N6o)
Many participants in grassroots mobilizations falsely think that the expressive component of exercising free speech is the most important. That view, however, gives too much power to the media institutions that have already [declared their allegiance](https://substack.com/redirect/23c31950-23f5-43e2-93bd-614a09de4051?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) with capital, and increasingly disregard grassroots mobilization for precisely that reason.
Ultimately, the greater value of local mobilization is the opportunity to find other neighbors who share (at least some of) your concerns. Especially in the digital age, as [community grows more and more difficult](https://substack.com/redirect/b08bdbb6-52a1-488f-be65-cc1b86a841a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to cultivate, meeting your neighbors in person to discover common cause has become an essentially revolutionary act in itself.
Beyond helping any participant feel connected and more supported, those connections within local networks can [build the capacity for resilience](https://substack.com/redirect/2d6ef777-079c-47fc-bf27-0301fc26ca4c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the face of everything from a natural disaster to a deployment of federal goon squads in your community.
As the litany of threats confronting our shared future continue to proliferate, building those local networks might be among the most effective ways we can each prepare for whatever lies in store in the days and years ahead.

Removing Wilcox from the NLRB basically represented union busting at a structural scale, impeding new unions from forming in the first place and hanging workers out to dry. At the same time, the right wing has also mounted two parallel assaults on labor: Trump has nominated union busting figures to fill Wilcox's seat on the NLRB, while business leaders are challenging its constitutionality before . As explained by : The NLRB hears and settles disputes between workers and employers, but Wilcox's departure deprived it of a quorum, making it unable to hear cases. Effectively, employers [are] free to violate already-weak labor law at will knowing there [is] no NLRB to penalize them. Trump has since tapped two nominees to staff the Board and return its function, but that might be worse in the long run. His first nominee is the former chief counsel to Trump's appointed NLRB Chairman, and the other built his career at the notorious union-busting firm Morgan, Lewis & Bockius LLP. These appointments might not even matter, as Trump's favorite billionaires have advanced a lawsuit through the hard right 5th Circuit court arguing the NLRB is unconstitutional. The case is on its way to Republican-controlled Supreme Court, where our unelected Harry Potter impersonators might complete conservatism's near-century-long campaign to scrap the New Deal. I'll leave aside for now the profound frustration raised by workers who supported Trump . We could, after all, discuss the president's until the end of time. For now, the point is that recent attacks on the right of organize have bloodied a sector that was already on its heels, hollowed out by longstanding trends in the economy including the corporate outsourcing that drove so much manufacturing abroad (and into prisons, where Americans by the millions 160 years after the Union victory in the Civil War). A post shared by Show solidarity today --and build your local network** Today, Americans are in hundreds of communities across the country to show solidarity with labor. As by the Guardian : Nearly 1,000 "worker over billionaire" protests are being planned in all 50 states starting this weekend as part of a Labor Day week of action organized by labor unions and advocacy groups in opposition to the Trump administration's policies. The actions include marches and rallies in cities such as and , a Labor Day parade in , rallies in Palmer, , Freeport, , and a planned protest at the state capitol in Honolulu, . The protests are organized by the AFL-CIO, the largest federation of labor unions in the US, and dozens of partner organizations, including Public Citizen, Indivisible, Democracy Forward, MoveOn and Patriotic Millionaires. 'This is about organic, grassroots organizing, and we intentionally wanted it to be outside of Washington DC, because that's where the impacts are being felt,' Liz Shuler, president of the AFL-CIO, said. 'Whether it's teachers or nurses or construction workers, they're all determined to stand up and fight back, because they're experiencing the cuts, they're experiencing the change in policies, they're experiencing the attacks of this White House on their unions, and so they're determined to make their voices heard and mobilize to fight forward regardless of what's happening around us, no matter the obstacles.' I'll be joining a gathering in my community today, and encourage you to . Many participants in grassroots mobilizations falsely think that the expressive component of exercising free speech is the most important. That view, however, gives too much power to the media institutions that have already with capital, and increasingly disregard grassroots mobilization for precisely that reason. Ultimately, the greater value of local mobilization is the opportunity to find other neighbors who share (at least some of) your concerns. Especially in the digital age, as to cultivate, meeting your neighbors in person to discover common cause has become an essentially revolutionary act in itself. Beyond helping any participant feel connected and more supported, those connections within local networks can in the face of everything from a natural disaster to a deployment of federal goon squads in your community. As the litany of threats confronting our shared future continue to proliferate, building those local networks might be among the most effective ways we can each prepare for whatever lies in store in the days and years ahead. name: Josh Gottheimer
subject: Labor Day --
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOSH GOTTHEIMER FOR CONGRESS
body:
# [#] Team,

Happy Labor Day!

I hope you’re taking the time to enjoy this holiday weekend, while also keeping in mind why we celebrate.

Today is a celebration of the hard-working men and women who have built our state and country up. We come together to celebrate the labor movement and recommit to fight for the ideals it promises. They represent Jersey Values, and I am proud to stand with them in Congress.

I have and will always support good-paying union jobs and strong labor standards.

I’m honored to represent the hard-working people of Jersey in Washington and continue to fight for them.

Yours,

Josh
Josh Gottheimer represents New Jersey’s Fifth Congressional District. Josh is in Congress to fight for lower taxes and Jersey Values. He’s working across the aisle to find real solutions for New Jersey families and is actively fighting against the ideological gridlock in Washington. clean_body: [#] Team, Happy Labor Day! I hope you’re taking the time to enjoy this holiday weekend, while also keeping in mind why we celebrate. Today is a celebration of the hard-working men and women who have built our state and country up. We come together to celebrate the labor movement and recommit to fight for the ideals it promises. They represent Jersey Values, and I am proud to stand with them in Congress. I have and will always support good-paying union jobs and strong labor standards. I’m honored to represent the hard-working people of Jersey in Washington and continue to fight for them. Yours, Josh Josh Gottheimer represents New Jersey’s Fifth Congressional District. Josh is in Congress to fight for lower taxes and Jersey Values. He’s working across the aisle to find real solutions for New Jersey families and is actively fighting against the ideological gridlock in Washington. If you'd like to support his campaign click here [] , if you'd like to unsubscribe click here. [] Thank you for being on Team Gottheimer. DONATE [] DONATE MONTHLY [] VOLUNTEER [] Prefer to donate by mail? Make checks payable to Josh Gottheimer for Congress PO Box 584 Ridgewood, NJ 07451 [] [] []

[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=172500850&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcyNTAwODUwLCJpYXQiOjE3NTY3NTQ2MDAsImV4cCI6MTc1OTM0NjYwMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.BqetP71vt0Cs3Vl1x5Ya-sEhJHbA6MbIgHLziXgU-lE)
It's been eleven days since my husband was taken.
Here's how it's going.
* They moved him to the pod, with the other low-risk detainees. Thank GOODNESS - alhamdulillah. He now has social contact, witnesses, and access to dimness for sleeping. No longer does he suffer in solitary confinement under 24/7 bright light. But he was there for **five nights and six days.**
* In the first eight days he was there, one time dinner was _two slices of bread._ Another time, dinner was served at 2 am. Another time, dinner was moldy, and they turned off everyone's communications for 24 hours while they got rid of the evidence. When he first arrived, they made him sign that he received four jumpsuits, but they only issued him one.
* I told this - about the horrible food, food that it would be a crime to feed to _animals_ \- to folks who already organize to support detainees at that facility. They said "Yeah, we know." This is nothing new.
* Talk about graft... GEO Group is charging the US Government plenty o'money for those meals - and _four_ jumpsuits - at probably 300% markup. We pay for them with taxpayer money. Which goes into private pockets. Specifically, _Stephen Miller 's_ pocket - he's a massive shareholder in GEO Group. **Personally profiting from these crimes against humanity.** At our expense.

* On Friday morning, Zahid had his expedited hearing with the immigration judge, considering whether to release him on bond. **Over 50 people came out in support** \- thank you, every one of you, whether you were there in person or sending support from afar.
* The judge, who only received our side's evidence at the hearing itself, said she needed to take a few more days to review the case before making a determination. We expect to know more by the end of this week.
* The court **was unwilling to accept Zahid 's filing from me **on Thursday, the day before the hearing, when I went to submit it. They said I was a "third party" (eg., not him and not his lawyer). I was literally standing at the Court Clerk's filing window and she _handed it back to me._**** That meant that the only way to get our evidence to the judge at all, was at the hearing itself.
* Those extra days are actually a good thing, for us. We were able to see, at the hearing, what was missing and what she was looking for - so we immediately crafted a Motion to Supplement the Record and got it into FedEx that afternoon, so it'll be on her desk first thing Tuesday. We gave her a stronger jurisdictional argument, and some of Zahid's 9th Circuit filings, for context.
* Then, over the weekend, we realized that there was more content that she needed - specifically, she needed to see evidence that:
* USCIS was still treating Zahid as "eligible for naturalization" by issuing Interview Notices in early August - at the same time as ICE claimed he has a "final order of removal" such that they could abduct him immediately after that interview.
* That's a highly _unusual_ and _inconsistent_ posture on the part of the opposition. Legally speaking, that should undermine the credibility of their arguments.
* The "final order of removal" they're referring to came from **2008.** Yes, a full 17 years ago. Before I graduated high school. It has:
1\. Been actively contested in court for those entire 17 years;
2\. Been overturned by a judge (in 2018) after a full decade of review on this case;
3\. Never been reissued or "served" clearly under current conditions; and
4\. Was based on minor issues - a plea-deal misdemeanor in Australia in the 1990s that Zahid proactively disclosed, and an alleged "false claim to citizenship" that expert evidence in the legal record proves, he never made.
* Weighing this old, contested, "pretextual"* order from 2008, against Zahid's honorable, decorated military service, his deep roots and family ties, and his 25 years of peaceful, lawful, contributive residence... well, it should be clear to any fair-minded judge what decision to make.

As part of this second filing, drafted over the weekend, we're including an affidavit by Ann, his former wife, who has known him since the year 2000 and was his 24/7 driver and companion for almost all of that time.
Ann is the only other eyewitness to the full sequence of events, and here's why that matters:
**During Zahid 's military service, he was approached by intelligence agencies and asked to become an informer. **
This was shortly after 9/11. (Zahid joined _before_ 9/11, in early 2001.)
I'm 33. I'm _just_ old enough to remember how much things changed for American Muslims, and Muslims in America, after that horrible event. Surveillance, suspicion, baseless imprisonment, racism, direct violence (against both Muslims and Sikhs), seizure of property, travel issues (the "terrorist watch list") - not to mention the USA PATRIOT Act that laid the groundwork for the surveillance state... not to mention, even, the creation of the Department of Homeland Security. (That wasn't a thing, before.) **And ICE.**
For Zahid, and other Muslims in the military, the sudden rise in anti-Muslim hate was terrible. Just a few incidents I know of personally:
* After a weekend trip to Vancouver, BC, Zahid and Ann were stopped at the border on the way home. They were pulled over to "Secondary Inspection" by US Border Patrol and interrogated under humiliating conditions for many hours. The officer directly accused Zahid of faking his Military ID - of making it in his basement - because "there are no Muslims in the military." (There were over 40,000 at that time.)
* One afternoon, Zahid was quietly meditating in the local mosque, when a rock was thrown through the window and hit him on the back. Zahid, who was still young, strong, and uninjured, chased after the assailant and made a citizen's arrest in the alley out back.
* One of Zahid's fellow soldiers, a friend to this day, was on a training run with his brothers-in-arms, sweating on a hot day, when he was given a bottle to drink (by the people he was told to trust with his life). That bottle contained not water, but _bleach._ The scars - internal scarring in his chest, and psychological ones - remain to this day.

These and other incidents - and I could tell you more... \- convinced Zahid that he should not trust the intelligence agencies that wanted him to work for them. They offered him everything - multiple six-figure salary, prestige, comfort, travel, flexibility, benefits, citizenship..... he would have had it made for life.
**But on principle, he refused.** He wanted no part of the surveillance and targeting of innocent people in his community. They told him "just tell us a name, who's a troublemaker there at your mosque?" And he knew, his career would be made _on that back of that person 's life being ruined._
So he said no.
And I love him forever for it.
And they turned against him in an instant.
First, they harassed him and his wife, Ann. They called persistently in the middle of the night, directly and aggressively telling him to come work for them. Ann was approached by strangers in the grocery story, saying "he'll listen to you, just tell your husband to come work for us, don't you want a comfortable life?" They were followed on dark nights on mountain passes - even targeted physically by those vehicles. Strange vehicles parked on their street and told the neighbors they were "investigating child endangerment" when there were no children in the neighborhood at that time. Even as late as 2019, their former neighbors (from their old home) said the FBI was still asking around about Zahid.
Then - after, fed up, Zahid finally told them to fuck off - they told him **his citizenship wouldn 't be forthcoming, **and they would** "make his life hell."**
--_A side note: my husband was consistently rated an "exceptional" soldier with "leadership potential." He's brilliant. He speaks nine languages. He's sincerely patriotic and driven to serve. He's culturally fluent in regions of key strategic interest to the United States: Pakistan borders Afghanistan, Iran, China, and India (and shares many cultural similarities with Iraq). I have no difficulty believing they wanted him this badly._

(*) It was after he refused to work for the agencies, that the government denied his application to naturalize, and found him "eligible for deportation" on spurious grounds. "Pretextual" grounds - meaning, they'll come up with any excuse, any minor issue, any "pretext" to delay or deny approval, even when an applicant has done everything right. This is under a program called [CARRP](https://substack.com/redirect/eebf4488-9547-4df9-922d-82c8febebfda?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), that Congress never authorized, and that has trapped tens of thousands of honest and peaceful people in legal limbo with no way to address the "security concerns" held against them.
**Zahid 's case, over the past ~20 years, shows 15 of the 19 characteristics legal practitioners use to tell when someone has been "CARRP'd."**
And in the case of _Wagafe v. Biden_ \- which the ACLU pursued for 12 years, until finally in January 2025 a federal judge found CARRP illegal - the government's own evidence revealed that at least 18 people have been "CARRP'd" and had their applications held in "national security" limbo **for over 20 years**. Zahid is one.
* * *
Sometimes people ask about his injuries.
They happened long before I knew him, but here's what I have reason to believe I know:
* There were multiple injuries that occurred in close timing. Culture in the military - especially at the time - was to "push through it" as much as possible. He did not have time to recover from one before the next one.
* One of these injuries, possibly the worst one, was a trampling episode. On night training, he and his fellow soldiers were out in full battle-rattle (think, each one carrying almost 200 lbs of gear). The transport truck stopped, and they all started leaping down. Zahid slipped and fell. The others couldn't stop fast enough.
* That should be more than enough to explain the broken lower back, broken neck, and traumatic brain injury (splitting, weeks-long migraines, etc) that led to his medical retirement. But there's more.
* He has tried to forget this memory, but I have seen documentation from earlier in his life. **One night, some of his soldiers and/or superiors abducted him, took him to an empty bunker, and beat him violently**. Mercilessly. Saying things like "You know what you did." and "Tell us what you know." Treating him like a genuine, honest-to-god terrorist they had to torture the truth out of.
* _He is not the only one I know of**personally** who was treated this way. _
* When he woke up the next morning - tied to the bed, in a disused bunker - his back was injured so severely he has never recovered, and uses a wheelchair to this day.

* * *
Let me take a moment to ask, **what kind of country treats its loyal soldiers like this?**
Leaving aside, for a moment, the question that I came to when the photos from Abu Ghraib Prison in Iraq came to light, where our soldiers were torturing innocent people and making crude humor out of it, then documenting their crimes.... _what kind of country treats**anyone** like this?_
We know - those of us who take an honest look at our own history - that the expansionist, white-supremacist origins of this country **never keep their promises to black and brown people**. not Native Americans. not enslaved Africans. not Japanese, for that matter. Not modern-day communities of color, as Trump's attack on DEI clearly demonstrates.
**" Liberty and Justice for All"** was always meant to be a code word. To be understood to apply only to white, Christian, English-speaking, property-owning men, more or less.
(For a deeper dive into this line of thinking - and it's a very useful one, if uncomfortable - take a look at this article on "[racial aristocracy](https://substack.com/redirect/ef855023-db30-43ce-8466-7730b5b5e0ce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" and see if it doesn't explain almost everything about our world today.)
BUT.
Those fundamental ideals of America:
Liberty and Justice for All.
Inalienable Rights to Life, Liberty, and the Pursuit of Happiness.
Government Of, By, and For the People.
Lifting our Lamp Beside the Golden Door.
...have been taught to so many generations of us now, from such a beautifully diverse and ethnically integrated cross-section of humanity, that **those of us who take those principles seriously,**_**outnumber**_**those of our "fellow Americans" who don't.**
I believe, and will stake my family's life on that belief, that **We the People** still means something real.
Maybe more real than ever.
My husband is wrongfully detained.
Him and....how many other tens of thousands of people in this country?
How many millions?
If we're going to find a way forward out of all this, it's going to start with getting really, really honest about how we got here,
and what the people in power, who don't want to share it, and who use it to abuse us...
are really up to. And why.
And if your mind is opening in new ways, let me offer this: anxiety, fear, and hatred of people who are different, is no fun. It's not necessary. It's not beneficial. It doesn't make your life better. It doesn't make your kids safer. The money it makes you, secretly makes you more miserable. It just makes things worse, for everyone, everywhere.
My personal vision is for everyone on earth to be able to climb a fruit tree on a long afternoon with a child they adore, and eat fruit together, and laugh together, and climb down and go to dinner with people they love and belong with, drinking clean water, resting peacefully and safely overnight, with good things to do the next morning.
We have a lot to dismantle, before that can be our reality.
Will you join me?

To be understood to apply only to white, Christian, English-speaking, property-owning men, more or less. (For a deeper dive into this line of thinking - and it's a very useful one, if uncomfortable - take a look at this article on "" and see if it doesn't explain almost everything about our world today.) BUT. Those fundamental ideals of America: Liberty and Justice for All. Inalienable Rights to Life, Liberty, and the Pursuit of Happiness. Government Of, By, and For the People. Lifting our Lamp Beside the Golden Door. ...have been taught to so many generations of us now, from such a beautifully diverse and ethnically integrated cross-section of humanity, that those of us who take those principles seriously,outnumberthose of our "fellow Americans" who don't. I believe, and will stake my family's life on that belief, that We the People still means something real. Maybe more real than ever. My husband is wrongfully detained. Him and....how many other tens of thousands of people in this country? How many millions? If we're going to find a way forward out of all this, it's going to start with getting really, really honest about how we got here, and what the people in power, who don't want to share it, and who use it to abuse us... are really up to. And why. And if your mind is opening in new ways, let me offer this: anxiety, fear, and hatred of people who are different, is no fun. It's not necessary. It's not beneficial. It doesn't make your life better. It doesn't make your kids safer. The money it makes you, secretly makes you more miserable. It just makes things worse, for everyone, everywhere. My personal vision is for everyone on earth to be able to climb a fruit tree on a long afternoon with a child they adore, and eat fruit together, and laugh together, and climb down and go to dinner with people they love and belong with, drinking clean water, resting peacefully and safely overnight, with good things to do the next morning. We have a lot to dismantle, before that can be our reality. Will you join me? You 're currently a free subscriber to "It's a Matter of Integrity" by Melissa Chaudhry. To support the mission and access the full experience, upgrade your subscription. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Melissa Chaudhry 6727 Martin Luther King Jr Way S, Suite M, #1117 Seattle, WA 98118 ? ?

name: Eric Swalwell
subject: Labor Day is more than a long weekend
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Swalwell For Congress
body:
Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District.
https://ericswalwell.com [https://ericswalwell.com]
Hey there —

I know it can be easy to just see Labor Day as a long weekend, the last breath of Summer, or even just a day with some really great sales, but we can’t forget what this day is really about.

Labor Day is a reminder of what working people have fought and sacrificed for to create safer workplaces, better working conditions, and fairer wages. This is a moment to look back at the strength and determination of workers then, and bring it into our advocacy today.

As the wealth gap widens and wages remain stagnant, we have to take inspiration from the fight of workers before us.

CHIP IN [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-Sept2025-FR-September?&refcode=E14961-20250901-LaborDay-SB-Other&amount=25]

In solidarity,

Eric

Believe me, writing this letter was not my first choice...

_As a matter of fact, it’s my _last_ _resort_. _

**You see, I’m writing to you as a man who’s run out of time, resources, and options ahead of the TRIAL OF MY LIFE.**

---
_No viable pardon from the President.
No protection.
No cavalry coming over the hill.
_

But even though the most powerful man in the country legally cannot step in to save me, the most powerful _force_ absolutely can: _THE AMERICAN PEOPLE_.

So, with my back against the wall, I’m turning to the one thing that can save me:**_the collective voices of MILLIONS of patriots like _YOU_._**

name: Chris Ahuja
subject: This Labor Day, We Honor Working Families
body:
People over profits. Always.

| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=ae768c7ca9&e=5a6ee82cdb)
| | | | | | Dear Peter,**Today, we celebrate the workers who built America:** the teachers, nurses, autoworkers, caregivers, service workers, and so many others who keep our country moving forward every single day.**Labor Day isn’t just a long weekend.** It’s a reminder of the generations of workers and unions who fought for the 8-hour workday, weekends, safer workplaces, and fair pay. And it’s a call to keep fighting because too many families are still struggling to make ends meet while corporate profits soar.**I’m running for Congress to make sure working people come first.** That means standing shoulder-to-shoulder with unions, protecting the right to organize, and ensuring every worker earns a living wage with dignity and respect.**This Labor Day, let’s recommit to building an economy that works for all of us, not just the wealthy few.****In solidarity,
**[****](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=d38aa7dbd9&e=5a6ee82cdb)**Chris Ahuja****Democrat for Congress, CD-32**

---
| [Donate](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=8b9c9b7023&e=5a6ee82cdb)

name: Keith
subject: Happy Labor Day
disclaimer_text: paid for by the Keith Ellison for Attorney General committee, PO Box 17224, Minneapolis, MN 55417
body:
Peter,

Labor Day is a powerful reminder of the debt we owe to the American labor movement.

Whether it’s the house we live in, the services we rely on, overtime pay, minimum wage, the weekend itself, or the countless other protections we enjoy — we can thank organized Labor.

I’ve always believed that America is strongest when our country’s workers are honored, paid well, and treated fairly. The Labor movement is fighting every day to make that happen, and I’m honored to stand with them — today and every day.

In solidarity,

Keith Ellison

Attorney General Keith Ellison is the People’s Lawyer, fighting to help people afford their lives and live with safety, dignity, and respect.

CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em250901ke?refcode=other]

clean_body: Peter, Labor Day is a powerful reminder of the debt we owe to the American labor movement. Whether it’s the house we live in, the services we rely on, overtime pay, minimum wage, the weekend itself, or the countless other protections we enjoy — we can thank organized Labor. I’ve always believed that America is strongest when our country’s workers are honored, paid well, and treated fairly. The Labor movement is fighting every day to make that happen, and I’m honored to stand with them — today and every day. In solidarity, Keith Ellison Attorney General Keith Ellison is the People’s Lawyer, fighting to help people afford their lives and live with safety, dignity, and respect. name: Deb Haaland
subject: What Labor Day means to New Mexicans
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
We know what’s at stake for working people.
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=4167%2E400544%2EjWtUCm)
Hi, Happy Labor Day!
Today, we celebrate the people who power this country and give their all so their families and communities can thrive.
We also remember the generations who fought for dignity on the job. The ones who faced down pink slips and picket lines so their kids could go to college. The ones who made it possible for our grandparents and parents to work hard and provide a good life for their families. We stand on their shoulders.
[****](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250901_1_laborday?amount=5&refcode=em20250901-4167&refcode2=4167_400544_jWtUCm&akid=4167%2E400544%2EjWtUCm)_I recently joined UA Local 412, families, and friends for an early Labor Day celebration.
Solidarity, labor pride, and hard work bands this community together._
**I’m running for governor because I believe New Mexico’s future belongs to working people** — not billionaires like Donald Trump or CEOs who’ve never punched a clock in their lives.
But right now, Trump and his allies are attacking the very foundations of the labor movement. He’s gutting workplace protections, slashing healthcare and school funding, and doing everything he can to break unions from coast to coast.
Meanwhile, our campaign is proudly endorsed by SEIU, one of the leading unions for service industry workers in America. They know I’ll never stop fighting for:
🟢 Safe working conditions
🟢 Better wages and lower costs
🟢 Healthy schools with better outcomes and healthcare that working families can count on
🟢 A clean energy economy built on union jobs
This campaign is powered by people like you — educators, janitors, farmworkers, small business owners, students, and retirees who believe in the promise of a better New Mexico.
**We support working people. We _are_ working people.**
Labor built this country. Labor keeps it running. And I’ll never forget where I come from — or who I’m fighting for.
In solidarity,
Deb
P.S. We need to win the New Mexico Governor’s race to keep up the fight for working people, but the GOP is eyeing this seat. **[Will you chip in $5 now to help us organize across the state and lift up every working New Mexican?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250901_1_laborday?amount=5&refcode=em20250901-4167&refcode2=4167_400544_jWtUCm&akid=4167%2E400544%2EjWtUCm)**
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_footer?refcode=em20250901-1&t=2&refcode2=4167_400544_jWtUCm&akid=4167%2E400544%2EjWtUCm)**
* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails-dh/?t=3&akid=4167%2E400544%2EjWtUCm)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/update-your-information-dh/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.debhaaland.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-dh/?t=4&akid=4167%2E400544%2EjWtUCm)

Email us: [info@debhaaland.com](mailto:info@debhaaland.com)
PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO

body:
The Left hates what this hat represents
What it represents is more powerful than you realize

| | | [](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=877563770a8649f49a9c124a9b1b62ac&_e=O5ARSm1VpFPHtKvzhAmKgd-HCxveMiEEzjNiAwSVHA-woNTybOWRcd-ANzp13FUm2wWJIvwDTEq0VG6zQAUhq-mTCbTfWUKOK_QaVzQV2Q1MhliQ3gRxwoJneBd1-vCAxI5IlyBNJoMy5OczUd1T2X1ua6FS-f8qSIPOk5rEsCIv4GCkD0AxRKgXS2JFqTXX-6lXmYeJ0jzZ50uPlpbsczZg-vj-sK_HrZNMfre8GmkufJhYb7fmg_gARqWKmCyUgS1uCizrN62z7Ou_yl2N-xfSiApvMEtQNLIvYoKobWTrq2xlBRWJ4VAZyjGjK5oc5k00dNF1Kiv_NCuEmRDK1o07ZIjd0VSUQZIDssJJMKKskTG4-cDV0uvAnURdxE8SxcE2cpFipEqMWi7agkUlgTdPnLf0GDjaz-mwqum9thPVLlnX7M7rbEp7hAUB9TPas-jJ1YK8nt5wiPaORE9kcUJ2HtoAANJ1k7P9MeOQrHGf5CTQ2RIAu5r8ikSm-Y3HZQEQDieI5wv2Z8hi8b2udw%3D%3D)
---
Peter,
---
I'll cut straight to the point... I want to send you a FREE limited-edition "47" hat...The same hat college students have literally begged for during our campus tour stops.
---
[](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=877563770a8649f49a9c124a9b1b62ac&_e=O5ARSm1VpFPHtKvzhAmKgd-HCxveMiEEzjNiAwSVHA-woNTybOWRcd-ANzp13FUm2wWJIvwDTEq0VG6zQAUhq-mTCbTfWUKOK_QaVzQV2Q1MhliQ3gRxwoJneBd1-vCAxI5IlyBNJoMy5OczUd1T2btbgHUhXYma8EfCttObPDTP5oHBR1Ibo0RUedURROJBkQnDaA1zMynX-6i-vVor0Lo8E5u3uccqW8FZuWpt8iWX1tyeChbI7LA4czwMQWAyJ43DoKMJBxlzyVb1omRx9ndAD83XaEM_E4hqx65gr1t4V2lX1i4-_zNJkQSVqp3zMI9s1ThiF2fwrBKjIn4R7cgLzPZF3jRleyfEUHJYC8OGf5ATplffJA2Q42El8XZLIJbmUvKO8HREmK6qlPXYoFhSr0EP06X0wMxvg5-IEocIsnWn-3AlLtuu_pIvnm-fVNOonfGbRNFeXH5PGzFdzpRkqXPVhtS0GYkuP16fjicMuoW9UKym3PFbrsj2PUFHAmYm2IEEWEpL8QZAHGFrQg%3D%3D)
---
But this isn't just any hat... It's a symbol.A symbol of what's happening right now across college campuses in America. _Students are waking up... Chanting "USA!"... Waving flags... and standing up for faith, family, and freedom._Everywhere Turning Point USA goes, we're seeing record crowds -- thousands of students -- fired up to defend this country and reject the Left's lies.And guess what?**They 're PROUD to be American.** This isn't just a moment... It's not a mere "shift"... This is a generational REVOLUTION!**And Peter, I want YOU to be part of it.**_All I ask?_**Make a gift of ANY amount to help Turning Point USA reach even more young people ...**...and I'll send you a limited-edition "47" hat as my thank-you.
---
[ **Support Turning Point USA &**
**Claim Your “47” Hat**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=877563770a8649f49a9c124a9b1b62ac&_e=O5ARSm1VpFPHtKvzhAmKgd-HCxveMiEEzjNiAwSVHA-woNTybOWRcd-ANzp13FUm2wWJIvwDTEq0VG6zQAUhq-mTCbTfWUKOK_QaVzQV2Q1MhliQ3gRxwoJneBd1-vCAxI5IlyBNJoMy5OczUd1T2XOGi7VzSyZcs_FyTQy5zo3Jx-CbjFEj3Vx0knhb8-YulGq3DBYMbzfJ61hCGGP1K1ZvoF6x8QQCH8ogQQOwuFj2UokVlwI6Yh_zLlE7O_PlKQ32iUfO95hR88Y_VqSi4_g3Z584gqQ7gMlGiFB33vpxrc5kE7FFIvClDfw01fHRxczge_wXr8O_wwKReFnsAagxX_NzFQCpupd3twXwY2aQGyeI8feVWrjDv1BRZ0goCP4zzxpWT8R4R-9Eg1tnQYv5SvkeZuqFlrxUkGOOyt25HCwEieZGu_nt8_NYcB_exLeei_LDjpdB1ePW_RGcnHgXKOdaNxaJXglxvGfLgopmMDwrKwkh6Gstw5mK_bkXLBXJuAwHqsbiYGrKb6LBaw%3D%3D)
---
Peter, this isn't a gimmick... It's not another piece of "merch"... This hat means something.To every student who wears one -- it's a statement: _" I'm not afraid to stand up for this country."_ To every patriot like you who fuels this mission -- it's a reminder:_ "I helped wake up a generation. I helped build a movement."_
---
[](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=877563770a8649f49a9c124a9b1b62ac&_e=O5ARSm1VpFPHtKvzhAmKgd-HCxveMiEEzjNiAwSVHA-woNTybOWRcd-ANzp13FUm2wWJIvwDTEq0VG6zQAUhq-mTCbTfWUKOK_QaVzQV2Q1MhliQ3gRxwoJneBd1-vCAxI5IlyBNJoMy5OczUd1T2aDg6ji5Ki09FwCAVMhQ8aSHC1DDE25DrTsfS2v63GS5ydiLCqWs3ij-tWFkSp3oGeKqxkDrTNms0dnhPbFLPLo7gp2QIUQD3pPA6G2Ux6ZZYt3zEeJJ_YuhRtW0D-WsktS8uh4ZRonerP6XZoH3_mngaGQtoX4hp7Jq9Bu-wxV8RIizk8-cry7wQq7Y-THwmJOtbvr7ddYWeZLOzs2RoaYX1QIwMobLQa8VbN45XtL90FmR_0Va1PapdfAgpfR6FzV3QzNcRE0jmpgy14mbgoU25gcSezVu4kRiF46Fu_LgqPqu2WhBp6T0wxY8vudyOdOuBqMdf44FnskmF1kmCjTrTMYPmo6sizDYjDgu6C7UaKZjEksP-h_1FMFpZRAq5g%3D%3D)
---
**The "47" hat is a badge of honor for people who are saving this country.****So please -- make your first gift now to help Turning Point USA push this movement even further...**🔥 More campuses
🔥 More students
🔥 More hearts and minds changed _And I 'll send you your "47" hat as a symbol of the mission you've joined._

clean_body: The Left hates what this hat represents What it represents is more powerful than you realize | | | Peter, I'll cut straight to the point... I want to send you a FREE limited-edition "47" hat...The same hat college students have literally begged for during our campus tour stops. But this isn't just any hat... It's a symbol.A symbol of what's happening right now across college campuses in America. Students are waking up... Chanting "USA!"... Waving flags... and standing up for faith, family, and freedom.Everywhere Turning Point USA goes, we're seeing record crowds -- thousands of students -- fired up to defend this country and reject the Left's lies.And guess what?They 're PROUD to be American. This isn't just a moment... It's not a mere "shift"... This is a generational REVOLUTION!And Peter, I want YOU to be part of it.All I ask?Make a gift of ANY amount to help Turning Point USA reach even more young people ......and I'll send you a limited-edition "47" hat as my thank-you. Peter, this isn't a gimmick... It's not another piece of "merch"... This hat means something.To every student who wears one -- it's a statement: " I'm not afraid to stand up for this country." To every patriot like you who fuels this mission -- it's a reminder: "I helped wake up a generation. I helped build a movement." The "47" hat is a badge of honor for people who are saving this country.So please -- make your first gift now to help Turning Point USA push this movement even further...🔥 More campuses 🔥 More students 🔥 More hearts and minds changed And I 'll send you your "47" hat as a symbol of the mission you've joined. Let's win the American Culture War. Let's make Gen Z the MOST PATRIOTIC generation in our nation's history.Let's take this thing all the way. For America, Charlie Kirk Founder & CEO Turning Point USA P.S. The Left never thought this could happen. But it is... right now, in real time.And patriots like YOU are the reason why. 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044

✅ He already seized control of Washington, D.C.’s police force and deployed 800 National Guard troops — despite record-low crime.

name: Lt. Cmdr George Moraitis
subject: I won’t insult your intelligence.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by George Moraitis for Congress Committee
body:
I respect you way too much - so I’ll skip the games and get right to it:

| | | | Friend, I won’t insult your intelligence.**_

I respect you way too much — so I’ll skip the games and get right to it:_**

My name is George Moraitis. I’m a Navy veteran, retired Lieutenant Commander, and the Pro-Trump, Pro-Wall, & Pro-America conservative running in the most consequential race in the country.

---
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b42D/?__vbtrk=MzE0NzY3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzE0NzY3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
| | |
When I joined the Navy fresh out of college, I knew I’d be sent to the most dangerous places on earth to defend this country.

_One place I never thought I’d be is here — writing this message to you._

But experts say this race could decide control of the House — and I’m being out-raised fast.

My opponent’s being propped up by radical elites from coast to coast and they’ll spend millions to shut me up.

If this message doesn’t get the support we need, **we lose**. Plain and simple.

**_[I’m not asking for the world — just a few bucks today to keep my campaign alive.](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b42D/?__vbtrk=MzE0NzY3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzE0NzY3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_**

---
[GIVE JUST $5 TODAY](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b42D/?__vbtrk=MzE0NzY3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzE0NzY3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

You might think a few bucks here and there won't make a difference — but **it truly will decide if we win this race or we fold completely.**

If we win, we hold the line for our House Majority.

But if we lose, we hand the keys to the Radical Left and _our country is finished._

Please, don’t let that happen.

**_[This is it — our final chance to turn things around and I'm humbly pleading for your help.](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b42D/?__vbtrk=MzE0NzY3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzE0NzY3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_**

[GIVE JUST $5 TODAY](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b42D/?__vbtrk=MzE0NzY3OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzE0NzY3OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

I’ll never forget those who stepped up when it mattered most.

George Moraitis
Ret. Lt. Commander, U.S. Navy
GOP Candidate for FL-23

| | | Paid for by George Moraitis for Congress Committee
---
| | 1103 HAYS STREET
TALLAHASSEE, FL 32301
